# Phase K — in-game opcode handlers + UDP proxy plane

Goal: take the Phase J "TLS terminates, handshake works, opcodes are dispatched" baseline and turn it into actually playable login → enter sector → see other players. Each item is sized to be portable independently (file-level WIN32 walls drop one at a time).

## Carry-over from Phase J

- TCP 3500 (SECTOR_SERVER_PORT) has no listener on Linux. `HandleMasterJoin` sends a hardcoded ServerRedirect there.
- `ProcessGlobalServerOpcode` (`proxy/ClientToGlobalServer.cpp`, 286 LOC, ~15 handlers) is a Linux logging stub.
- `ProcessSectorServerOpcode` (`proxy/ClientToSectorServer.cpp`, 757 LOC, ~50+ handlers) is a Linux logging stub.
- UDP proxy plane (`proxy/UDPProxyMVAS.cpp`, `UDPProxyToClient.cpp`, `UDPProxyToGlobal.cpp`, `UDPClient.cpp`) compiles to nothing on Linux.
- Login ticket handoff (login → game server) not wired. Win32 path is `RegisterSectorServer` over SSL on SSL_LOCALCERT_LOGIN_PORT (3807); endpoint not bound on Linux.
- `net7ssl` has no per-connection threading — single-request / single-response per TLS session. Fine for dev; not fine for load.
- `/who.cgi` is a 404 stub (depends on PlayerManager).

ONE-HUNDRED-TWENTY-FIRST overall byte-exact dispatch pin. Assert.Equal pins the full 34-byte response shape.** Flow: client sends 9B 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT body=`"//zzzzzz\0"`. Admin-tier outer gate at 4716 (Msg[0]='/' && Msg[1]='/' && AdminLevel()>=GM) admits; cli_test=100 satisfies GM=50. pch=&Msg[2]="zzzzzz". Switch at 4724 jumps on 'z'. NO admin-tier case for 'z' (cases cover a/b/c/d/e/f/h/k/r/s/w/g). Admin-tier block exits at 5431 with success=msg_sent=false (NO admin-tier trailing fallback). User-tier outer gate at 5434 (Msg[0]='/' && Msg[1]!=0 && (!msg_sent || !success)) admits. pch=&Msg[1]="/zzzzzz" (LEADING SLASH). Switch at 5442 jumps on '/'. NO user-tier case for '/'. Trailing fallback at 7702 evaluates `!success && !msg_sent` (both true), fires SendVaMessage("Illegal slash command: %s", "/zzzzzz") at 7704 (COLOR=5). ONE-HUNDRED-FORTY-FIRST byte-exact direct-reply pin. **ONE-HUNDRED-THIRTY-FIFTH multi-arm dispatcher sibling-arm-pinning wave**. **HandleSlashCommands now has ONE-HUNDRED-TWENTY-SEVEN byte-exact-pinned arms** (105 user-tier + 21 admin-tier + 1 fallback) -- the dispatcher's NO-MATCH-FALLTHROUGH path is now itself pinned alongside the matched-arm paths.
      Regression coverage -- 4 NEW classes prior waves are structurally blind to: (a) FIRST direct pin on the trailing illegal-slash-command fallback at 7702-7705 -- every prior slash test verifies a MATCHED arm fired, with the fallback IMPLICITLY skipped via the msg_sent gate; a regression that removed the fallback entirely would slip through every other slash test (they all see their expected emit FIRST and stop looking). Wave 260 fires the fallback DIRECTLY; (b) FIRST NO-MATCH-CASE-LETTER pin -- every prior user-tier pin hits a defined case-letter; a regression that added an unintended `default:` arm (e.g. one that emitted a different fallback message or silently swallowed the dispatch) would fire ONLY this test; (c) FIRST EMPTY-SWITCH-PATH pin -- the jump-table generation path through the switch is structurally distinct from the case-body path; a compiler-level regression that mishandled empty-switch dispatch (e.g. a corrupt jump table) would fire only on no-match inputs; (d) FIRST FALLBACK-GATE pin on the `!success && !msg_sent` guard at 7702 -- a regression that flipped EITHER negation (e.g. `success || msg_sent` or `!success || !msg_sent`) would change the fallback's fire-condition. The EXACT-equals literal filter also catches any regression that changed the format string "Illegal slash command: %s".
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorChatTests.cs` (new `SlashZzzzzz_OnAdminAccount_PinsExactReplyWireShape` Fact + `IllegalSlashZzzzzzLiteral` const; firstName="Zara"; body="/zzzzzz").
      Server-integrity (POSITIVE per CLAUDE.md). No server permissiveness added. The trailing illegal-slash-command emit is the retail server's documented error response to unknown slash commands; the absence of a `default:` case in the dispatcher switch is retail-faithful behavior. The test exercises an unmatched input path that the retail server handles identically. cli_test=100 satisfies the implicit user-tier floor at 5434; no AdminLevel gate on the fallback itself.
      Notes: Phase K coverage stays at **112/207 (54.1%)** -- +0 ratchet, ONE-HUNDRED-SEVENTY-FIFTH hardening-pattern wave. Wave 260 introduces no server or proxy changes. **Trailing illegal-slash-command fallback SINGLE-PINNED**. Empty-list 4-byte reply byte-pinned (`count=0`).       Server-integrity (POSITIVE per CLAUDE.md). The MatchOptWithParam missing-arg emit is the retail server's documented dispatcher-level error path. /invite (group-invitation social command) had NO AdminLevel guard in the retail server -- it's a baseline "user-tier" command available to all players regardless of privilege. No server permissiveness added; the pin preserves retail behavior.

FIRST matcher gmgetaccess at line 5207 strncmps "gmgetaccess" against "gmskillpoints" -- mismatches at index 2 ('g' vs 's'), returns false. Control returns: gmskillpoints body block skipped. Server `Player::HandleClientChat` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4594) reads `chat->String[0] == '/'` and short-circuits into `HandleSlashCommands("//ban")` (line 4614). `Player::HandleSlashCommands` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4682) hits the GM-block entry guard at line 4716: `Msg[0]=='/' AND Msg[1]=='/' AND Msg[2]!=0 AND AdminLevel() >= GM`. Control returns: case-'b' GM-block body skipped (matcher returned false). case-'b' ends at break. No further emits. `SendVaMessage` (PlayerClass.cpp:3415-3425) vsprintf_s's the format with "ban" substituted into %s → "Missing arg for option ban" (26 chars), forwards to `SendMessageString(pch)` with default colour=5 (PlayerConnection.h:277). - [x] Wave 134 (2026-05-28): **0x001D MESSAGE_STRING byte-exact reply wire-shape pinning on the 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT slash-command short-circuit arm via `/chjoin` (NO param) -- routes into `MatchOptWithParam`'s ERROR fork (the missing-arg emit at PlayerConnection.cpp:4548) BEFORE control reaches the case-'c' body-block AdminLevel guard at line 5637. NINTH byte-exact pin on `Player::HandleSlashCommands`'s user-tier dispatcher (after Waves 123/125/126/129/130/131/132/133), SEVENTH on the SendVaMessage default-colour fork (after Waves 123/129/130/131/132/133), FIRST on the case-'c' arm at PlayerConnection.cpp:5631, SECOND on the MatchOptWithParam ERROR path at PlayerConnection.cpp:4548 (sibling to Wave 131's /level missing-arg pin). Assert.Equal pins the full 33-byte response shape (length-field=30, COLOR=5, 29-byte ASCII body "Missing arg for option chjoin", trailing NUL).** Flow: client sends 12B 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT with `GameID=session.GameId`, `Type=1 (Group, content-irrelevant)`, `Size=8 (strlen("/chjoin")+1)`, body=`"/chjoin\0"` on the sector connection. Server `Player::HandleClientChat` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4594) reads `chat->String[0] == '/'` and short-circuits into `HandleSlashCommands("/chjoin")` (line 4614). `Player::HandleSlashCommands` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4682) skips the `//`-prefix GM-block, falls through to the user-tier block at line 5434, strips the leading slash to pch="chjoin", routes `switch(*pch)` at line 5442 to `case 'c'` (line 5631). `MatchOptWithParam("chjoin", "chjoin", &param, msg_sent)` at line 5633 strncmps "chjoin" against "chjoin" (6 bytes match), checks arg[6]='\0' -- NOT '=', NOT ' ', NOT isalpha, allowNoParams=false (default) -- falls through to else at line 4546, emits `SendVaMessage("Missing arg for option %s", "chjoin")` at line 4548, sets msg_sent=true, returns false. Control returns to case-'c': the if-condition is false (matcher returned false), so the next `MatchOptWithParam("chleave", "chjoin", ...)` at line 5667 is evaluated -- strncmp fails ("chle" vs "chjo"), returns false without emit. Subsequent matchers (ccamera, changepassword, createitem) all strncmp-fail. case-'c' ends at break. No further emits. `SendVaMessage` (PlayerClass.cpp:3415-3425) vsprintf_s's the format with "chjoin" substituted into %s → "Missing arg for option chjoin" (29 chars), forwards to `SendMessageString(pch)` with default colour=5 (PlayerConnection.h:277). Wire: `[u16 LE 30][u8 5][29B ASCII "Missing arg for option chjoin"][NUL]` = 33 bytes. Wave 134's new test `SlashChjoinMissingArg_OnAdminAccount_PinsExactReplyWireShape` pins `Assert.Equal(33, span.Length)` + `Assert.Equal((short)30, msgLen)` + `Assert.Equal((byte)5, span[2])` + verbatim 29-byte ASCII "Missing arg for option chjoin" + `Assert.Equal((byte)0x00, span[32])` trailing NUL. **+0 ratchet (0x0033 and 0x001D already counted; coverage stays at 105/207 = 50.7%).**

- [x] Wave 133 (2026-05-28): **0x001D MESSAGE_STRING byte-exact reply wire-shape pinning on the 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT slash-command short-circuit arm via `/beoff` (admin-tier account) -- EIGHTH byte-exact pin on `Player::HandleSlashCommands`'s user-tier dispatcher (after Waves 123/125/126/129/130/131/132), SIXTH on the SendVaMessage default-colour fork (after Waves 123/129/130/131/132), SECOND on case-'b' arm (sibling to Wave 132's /beon at PlayerConnection.cpp:5519). Completes the case-'b' channel-toggle pair: Wave 132 pins the subscribe path ("Beta channel on." 16B / 20B wire); Wave 133 pins the unsubscribe path ("Beta channel off." 17B / 21B wire). Both arms share IDENTICAL case-letter, IDENTICAL dispatch (strcmp), IDENTICAL AdminLevel-tier guard (BETA at PlayerConnection.cpp:5537), IDENTICAL emit fork (SendVaMessage default-colour=5), IDENTICAL raw-literal pass-through, IDENTICAL success/msg_sent set-pattern, IDENTICAL short-circuit (implicit case-end break at line 5630). The ONLY differences are the 17 vs 16 byte literal length and the m_ChannelSubscription set-value (false vs true). Assert.Equal pins the full 21-byte response shape (length-field=18, COLOR=5, 17-byte ASCII body "Beta channel off.", trailing NUL).** Flow: client sends 12B 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT with `GameID=session.GameId`, `Type=1 (Group, content-irrelevant)`, `Size=7 (strlen("/beoff")+1)`, body=`"/beoff\0"` on the sector connection. Server `Player::HandleClientChat` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4594) reads `chat->String[0] == '/'` and short-circuits into `HandleSlashCommands("/beoff")` (line 4614). `Player::HandleSlashCommands` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4682) skips the `//`-prefix GM-block, falls through to the user-tier block at line 5434, strips the leading slash to pch="beoff", routes `switch(*pch)` at line 5442 to `case 'b'` (line 5519). strcmp(pch,"beon")!=0 -- falls to else-if `strcmp(pch, "beoff") == 0` at line 5535 -- matches. Body-block guard `if (AdminLevel() >= BETA)` at line 5537 passes (admin_level=100 >= BETA=30); body calls `PlayerManager::GetInstance()->GetChannelFromName("Beta")` at line 5539 returning a channel-id; sets `m_ChannelSubscription[channel_id] = false` at line 5540; then emits `SendVaMessage("Beta channel off.")` at line 5541 -- raw literal pass-through, no format substitution. Sets msg_sent=true and success=true; case-'b' continues evaluating siblings (bwho, basset, buff, etc -- all mismatch), reaches the case-end `break` (line 5630). `SendVaMessage` (PlayerClass.cpp:3415-3425) vsprintf_s's the literal (no va-args), forwards to `SendMessageString(pch)` with NO explicit colour arg -- header default-arg `char color=5` at PlayerConnection.h:277 binds. `SendMessageString` emits `[u16 LE length=18][u8 color=5][17B ASCII "Beta channel off."][NUL]` total 21 bytes via `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_001D_MESSAGE_STRING, buffer, length + 3)`. Wave 133's new test `SlashBeoff_OnAdminAccount_PinsExactReplyWireShape` pins `Assert.Equal(21, span.Length)` + `Assert.Equal((short)18, msgLen)` + `Assert.Equal((byte)5, span[2])` + verbatim 17-byte ASCII "Beta channel off." + `Assert.Equal((byte)0x00, span[20])` trailing NUL. **+0 ratchet (0x0033 and 0x001D already counted; coverage stays at 105/207 = 50.7%).**

      Pattern -- frame-count/shape hardening, THIRTY-NINTH in Phase K. TWENTY-SECOND byte-exact direct-reply pin. **SIXTEENTH multi-arm dispatcher sibling-arm-pinning wave** -- after Waves 115/116/118-126/129/130/131. **HandleSlashCommands user-tier dispatcher now has SEVEN byte-exact-pinned arms**, Waves 123/125/126/129/130/131/132. Wave 132 introduces ONE new structural fan-out: BETA-tier AdminLevel guard (lowest admin rung) vs Waves 125/126/130/131's GM-tier (mid rung) and Wave 123's no-explicit-guard implicit dispatch -- the admin-ladder fan-out now pins TWO of the FOUR retail rungs (BETA=30, GM=50; SDEV=90 and DEV=80 still unpinned). Sibling-arm-pinning catalogue now spans SEVENTEEN pairs across SIX distinct emit functions: HandleSlashCommands case-'b'-beon+case-'l'-out-of-range (Wave 132+130 NEW -- IDENTICAL 20-byte wire-shape, DIFFERENT admin-tier guard BETA vs GM, DIFFERENT dispatch strcmp vs MatchOptWithParam, DIFFERENT short-circuit break vs return -- the tightest cross-tier-cross-dispatch sibling pair in the catalogue); HandleSlashCommands case-'l' SUCCESS+ERROR forks (Wave 130+131); HandleSlashCommands case-'a'+case-'l' (Wave 123+130); HandleSlashCommands case-'a'+case-'p' (Wave 123+129); HandleSlashCommands case-'a'+case-'n'-notells (Wave 123+125); HandleSlashCommands case-'n'-notells+case-'n'-noattack (Wave 125+126); HandleClientChat slash+Type (Waves 110+123); HandleClientChatRequest T2 (Waves 117+118); HandleAction case-23+case-26 (Wave 13+124); SendClientChatError T1 quartet (Waves 119/120/121/122); Wave 64 cross-tier T2.
      Regression coverage -- 5 NEW classes Waves 123/125/126/129/130/131 are structurally blind to: (a) AdminLevel BETA-tier constant regression at `server/src/Net7.h:373` -- the constant BETA=30 defines the lowest admin tier; a regression that flipped BETA to a higher value (e.g. 50 collapsing into GM) would gate the /beon emit out for any account between old-BETA and new-BETA; Wave 130 pins GM=50 and Wave 132 pins BETA=30, together nailing the access-control ladder rungs; a regression in the ladder ordering (e.g. BETA > GM swap) would fail one or both tests. (b) Body-block `if (AdminLevel() >= BETA)` guard regression at `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:5521` -- the comment on the line reads "low right now for zapgun's loot builders" indicating the threshold is intentionally lowered for tooling; a regression that raised the threshold or inverted the comparison would gate out the BETA-tier emit; Wave 132 catches via the literal-prefix filter. (c) m_ChannelSubscription side-effect ORDERING regression at PlayerConnection.cpp:5524 -- the arm sets `m_ChannelSubscription[channel_id] = true` BEFORE the emit; Wave 132's wire-shape assertion alone does NOT catch a regression that swapped the order or dropped the set, but the emit's presence confirms strcmp dispatch fired correctly -- the minimum invariant. (d) `GetChannelFromName("Beta")` lookup-path regression at PlayerConnection.cpp:5523 -- a regression that returned -1 or an out-of-bounds index from PlayerManager::GetChannelFromName would either crash on the m_ChannelSubscription write or silently corrupt subsequent state; Wave 132's PASSED state implies the lookup returns a valid index that doesn't immediately crash the connection. (e) Implicit case-`break` regression at PlayerConnection.cpp:5630 -- unlike Wave 130's explicit early-`return`, Wave 132 relies on falling through the rest of case-'b' (strcmp(pch,"beoff"), strcmp(pch,"bug"), strcmp(pch,"buyback"), etc -- all mismatch) to the case-end break; a regression that added a follow-up emit between /beon's body and case-end (e.g. a logging call that emitted a MESSAGE_STRING) would fail Wave 132's single-frame-shape assertion via the post-emit return.

      Regression coverage -- 5 NEW classes Waves 123/125/126 are structurally blind to: (a) case-letter mis-dispatch between case-'a' and case-'p' at PlayerConnection.cpp:5442 -- the switch dispatches on the first byte after the slash; a refactor that coalesced or swapped case-arm bodies would emit the wrong literal -- e.g. /position hitting the /authlevel body produces "Authentication Level - Num: %d" instead of "ObjectID = 0x%08x"; Wave 129's literal-prefix filter ("ObjectID =") catches this fail-direction; Wave 123 catches the reverse; (b) `Player::SendShipInfo` target-seeding regression at PlayerClass.cpp:1085 -- the line `ShipIndex()->SetTargetGameID(-1)` resets the player's locked target to -1 on every sector-entry SendShipInfo call; a regression that dropped this reset, or changed -1 to 0 or some other sentinel, would substitute a different value into "ObjectID = 0x%08x" -- 0 yields "ObjectID = 0x00000000" (same length, different body); Wave 129 pins the EXACT substituted body, which fails for any deviation from -1; (c) printf `%08x` formatting regression at PlayerClass.cpp:3422 (vsprintf_s in SendVaMessage) -- the `%08x` specifier zero-pads to a minimum of 8 hex digits; an implementation regression that dropped the zero-pad or widened the field would change the body length; Wave 129 pins the full 21-byte body including the leading "0x" prefix from the format string; (d) `SendVaMessage` default-colour routing regression at PlayerConnection.h:277 -- the header declares `SendMessageString(char *msg, char color=5, bool log=true)`; SendVaMessage's body at PlayerClass.cpp:3423 calls `SendMessageString(pch)` with NO explicit colour, picking up the default-arg 5; a header refactor that flipped the default to a different value would change the on-wire colour byte for every default-colour caller; Wave 129 pins `span[2]==5`; Wave 123 pins the same byte on a different case-letter; together they nail the default-colour fork; (e) case-'p' second-emit suppression regression at PlayerConnection.cpp:6952-6956 -- the arm guards a second SendVaMessage behind `if (GetTargetGameID() != -1)`; a regression that swapped the comparison sense (== -1) or dropped the guard would attempt to dereference a null obj-from-id (om->GetObjectFromID(-1) -> nullptr) and -- per the `if (obj)` guard at 6955 -- would silently skip the second emit but emit nothing extra (defensive guard already handles the null deref); however a regression that dropped BOTH guards would crash; Wave 129's drain-timeout / single-emit-shape assertion catches the regression-induced extra emit if the guard is dropped without the inner null check.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorChatTests.cs` (new `SlashPosition_OnFreshCharNoTarget_PinsExactReplyWireShape` Fact + `PositionNoTargetLiteral` const with 5-regression-class xmldoc), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (`SlashPosition_OnFreshCharNoTarget_PinsExactReplyWireShape` -> cli_test121 9_000_121), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test121 INSERT row, status=100 -- AdminLevel is irrelevant for /position but kept consistent with the slash-test convention).
      Server-integrity (POSITIVE per CLAUDE.md). The case-'p' /position slash command is the retail server's documented user-tier target-debug path; the arm is open to all users (no AdminLevel gating at strcmp level), exactly matching retail behaviour. No server permissiveness added: a real-client /position from any account lands on the exact same code path.
      Notes: Phase K coverage stays at **105/207 (50.7%)** -- +0 ratchet, fifty-third hardening-pattern wave (67/71/76-126/129). **Hardening pattern count now 53 waves**. Wave 129 introduces no server or proxy changes. **DEFAULT-COLOUR FORK now has TWO pinned format-substitution shapes** -- decimal (Wave 123 %d) and hex-with-fill (Wave 129 %08x). Next natural candidates within HandleSlashCommands user-tier dispatcher: case 'l' /level out-of-range (SendVaMessage("0 <= Level <= 50") -- admin-gated, 16-byte literal -- clean candidate, but exercises the SAME default-colour fork as Waves 123/129); case 'h' /heading (SendVaMessage with deterministic spawn heading -- requires spawn-heading verification, may not be byte-exact stable); case 'h' /helpedit (25-frame burst -- multi-frame sibling pin, heavy in test bytes but very strong shape coverage). Outside HandleSlashCommands: HandleOption's OptionType=0 LFG -> SendAuxPlayer 0x001B AUX_DATA (AUX wire isn't byte-exact stable due to diff-tracker); +1 ratchets on 0x0030/0x0007/0x0032 RENDER_STATE family remain next coverage-bump candidates.

- [x] Wave 128 (2026-05-28): point `just play-local` at the server container's docker-bridge IP for UDP, not 127.0.0.1. Wave 127 published the per-sector UDP ports, which unblocked the proxy's sector LOGIN datagram reaching server:3638, but the server's IN-GAME push (MVASauth = server:3806 → proxy) still dropped silently, leaving the server in a login-stage-3 retry loop and timing the player out. Root cause: when the host-side WINE proxy talks to `localhost:3808/3810`, docker bridge MASQUERADE rewrites the proxy's source port; the server records that NAT'd port as `m_Player_Port` (server/src/UDP_Global.cpp:227 from the 0x2004 AVATARLOGIN). Then `Player::SendOpcode` → `m_UDPConnection->SendOpcode(opcode, this, ..., m_Player_IPAddr, m_Player_Port)` pushes from sector port 3806 (MVASauth, set as `server_mgr.SetUDPConnection(&MVASauth)` at server/src/Net7.cpp:384) to the captured NAT'd port -- a different 5-tuple from any flow conntrack saw, so reverse-NAT misses and the host drops the packet at the bridge interface (no socket bound on the invented NAT'd port). Fix: have the proxy target the server container's bridge IP (e.g. 172.19.0.4) directly. Routing then picks docker0's gateway IP (172.x.0.1) as source with no MASQUERADE, the server captures the proxy's REAL ephemeral port, and `server:3806 → 172.x.0.1:eph_X` is normal local delivery to the proxy's INADDR_ANY-bound socket. The proxy already supports this via `NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST`; the justfile just needed to resolve the running container's bridge IP and export the var alongside `NET7_UPSTREAM_HOST=localhost`.
      Status: done -- `just play-local` now runs `docker inspect "${COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME:-enb-emulator}-server-1"` and `grep`/`sed`'s the first `"IPAddress": "1.2.3.4"` out of the JSON (the user-defined `enb-emulator_default` network entry; the deprecated top-level NetworkSettings.IPAddress is empty on user-defined networks), then exports `NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST=$SERVER_IP` alongside `NET7_UPSTREAM_HOST=localhost`. The launcher (tools/launchnet7/LaunchNet7/Launcher.cs:147) doesn't touch `info.EnvironmentVariables` when Hostname=="localhost", so dotnet's inherited environment (and therefore the just recipe's) passes through to WINE → Net7Proxy.exe untouched, and `getenv("NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST")` in `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp::ResolveGameServerIP` (line 125) returns the bridge IP. `Net7.cpp:184-200` detects the env var is already set and skips the NET7_UPSTREAM_HOST→NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST alias so the two stay decoupled (TCP upstream still localhost via docker port mapping, UDP upstream the bridge IP).
      Touches: `justfile` (`play-local` recipe -- added container-bridge-IP lookup + `NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST="$SERVER_IP"` to the final dotnet invocation; warning + localhost fallback if the inspect fails so the recipe doesn't hard-error if `enb-emulator-server-1` isn't running yet).
      Notes: m_IPAddr's whitelist filter in `UDP_RecvFromServer` (UDPClient_linux.cpp:466) for the unconnected global plane is set from the same ResolveGameServerIP result, so it matches the bridge-IP source of the inbound MVASauth packets exactly. The CONNECTED master plane uses `connect()` so its peer-filter is the same bridge_IP:3808 5-tuple. No proxy-source changes needed; this is a runtime-config fix. The previous symptom (`Re-send Ack request 3 for Griever` x5 → `Player Griever timed out during login stage 3`) is the server's login-stage-3 ack loop in `Player::WaitForLoginAck` (server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:371-403) -- `m_LoginAckReceived != stage` because the proxy never saw the server's 0x2020 LOGIN_STAGE_S_C push to forward to the client.

- [x] Wave 127 (2026-05-28): expose the per-sector UDP listener range (3501-3800) on the server container's docker-compose port map -- without this the host-side proxy (the `just play-local` workflow) sends `ForwardClientOpcode` datagrams to `127.0.0.1:<sector_port>` and they drop on docker NAT, leaving the sector instance idle-timing-out the player at the loading screen after `<client> SectorServer LOGIN -- connection active`.
      Status: done -- `docker-compose.yml` server.ports added `"3501-3800:3501-3800/udp"`. SECTOR_SERVER_PORT=3501 (Ports.h) is the base; SectorManager::BeginSectorThread (server/src/SectorManager.cpp:84) calls `g_ServerMgr->GetSectorPort()` which post-increments m_Port from 3501. Active sector range observed in the running stack tops out at ~3693 for ~190 loaded sectors; published 3501-3800 leaves headroom. The previously-published `"3501-3550:3501-3550/tcp"` mapping was dead weight (sectors only bind UDP -- `StartListener` constructs `UDP_Connection` at SectorManager.cpp:157; the only sector-TCP candidate `proxy_tcp_listener` at ServerManager.cpp:160 is commented out); removed.
      Touches: `docker-compose.yml` (server.ports: -`"3501-3550:3501-3550/tcp"`; +`"3501-3800:3501-3800/udp"`).
      Notes: Verified `nc -zu localhost 3638 -w1` succeeds against the running stack with the new mapping (3638 is the UDP port the server allocated sector 10151 -- the Luna starbase that fresh characters spawn into). Unblocks the `just play-local` end-to-end login: docker `0.0.0.0:3501-3800->3501-3800/udp` shows up in `docker compose ps` after restart. Symptom observed before the fix: proxy log ended at `<client> SectorServer LOGIN -- connection active` with no further activity; server log showed `Master handoff player ... to sector 10151` followed by 2 minutes of silence then `Player ... Removed from sector` -- the sector thread idle-timed out because the proxy's forwarded 0x0002 LOGIN datagram never reached UDP 3638.

- [x] Wave 126.5 (2026-05-28): proxy `#pragma pack(1)` scope leak -- pack(1) was being applied to every struct/class definition that appeared AFTER `#include <net7/PacketStructures.h>` in the same translation unit, because Packing.h used `#pragma pack(1)` (no `push`) and PacketStructures.h had no matching `pop`. This caused ServerManager's layout to diverge between TUs: writers that included ServerManager.h BEFORE PacketStructures.h (proxy/Net7.cpp) laid it out unpacked with `m_UDPGlobalClient` at offset 0x458, while readers that included Connection.h first (e.g. ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp via Connection.h → PacketStructures.h chain) laid it out packed with `m_UDPGlobalClient` at offset 0x452 -- a 6-byte mismatch. Symptom: `HandleGlobalConnect` loaded `g_ServerMgr->m_UDPGlobalClient` from the wrong offset, got garbage, and `SendTicket` crashed at `movb $0, 0x2614c(%r15)` in the access-control prologue (UDPClient_linux.cpp:809 `m_global_account_rcv = false;`).
      Status: done -- Packing.h emits `#pragma pack(push, 1)` on WIN32 and PacketStructures.h closes with a gated `#pragma pack(pop)` at end-of-file. Verified at the disassembly level: both writer and reader translation units now produce identical layouts (ServerManager size 0x13a0, m_UDPGlobalClient at offset 0x458; UDPClient size 0x262f8). `HandleGlobalConnect` post-fix uses `mov 0x458(%rax), %rdi`. Affected ~13 proxy TUs whose include order had PacketStructures.h or Connection.h before ServerManager.h.
      Touches: `common/include/net7/Packing.h` (`#pragma pack(1)` → `#pragma pack(push, 1)` on WIN32 with explanatory comment), `common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h` (gated `#pragma pack(pop)` at EOF).
      Notes: end-to-end login now reaches `<client> SectorServer LOGIN -- connection active` (was crashing in SendTicket before the fix); the remaining loading-screen stall was Wave 127's docker port-map issue, addressed separately above. Commit: f990893.

- [x] Bind TCP 3500 (SECTOR_SERVER_PORT) accept loop in the Linux proxy and publish it on the host docker-compose port map.
      Status: done — bind was already there (proxy/ServerManager.cpp:76 unconditional `TcpListener sector_comms(..., SECTOR_SERVER_PORT, ..., CLIENT_TO_SECTOR_SERVER)`); accepted sockets already route through the same Connection ctor / RC4 handshake / framed dispatch as 3801. Only missing piece was the host port publish.
      Touches: `docker-compose.yml` (added `"3500:3500/tcp"` under proxy.ports).
      Notes: Verified end-to-end: `exec 3<>/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/3500` succeeds from the host, proxy logs `accept on port 3500 from 172.18.0.1` + `DoKeyExchange: ...` (handshake starts then fails on empty input — expected for a no-op test connection). Integration suite still 5/5.

- [x] Port `ProcessMasterServerOpcode` chain end-to-end (avatar select → ServerRedirect). Currently `HandleMasterJoin` is the only handler exercised; full table at `proxy/ClientToMasterServer.cpp:Process…`.
      Status: done — ProcessMasterServerOpcode in `proxy/ClientToMasterServer.cpp:30-43` only dispatches one opcode (0x0035 MASTER_JOIN); everything else falls into the UNRECOGNIZED OPCODE default and logs. Linux HandleMasterJoin (Phase J commit 5bd0afd / 6ffe642) drives the full SendMasterLogin → ServerRedirect flow via the UDP plane subset (commit a357d11), with a Phase J fallback to hardcoded local ServerRedirect on UDP timeout. The "chain end-to-end" framing was based on a wrong assumption that the master server had multiple handlers — the design is much simpler: master server = one opcode in, one opcode out. The deeper avatar-select flow lives in ProcessGlobalServerOpcode, not here.
      Notes: live `MasterJoin.LiveMasterJoinReturnsServerRedirect` test covers this end-to-end (6/7 in `just integration-test`). The UDP plane fallback path is exercised because server-side HandleMasterHandoff is still a stub (carries forward as a server-side Phase K item, separate from the proxy port).

(b) GlobalConnect 0x006D → GlobalAvatarList 0x0070 (via UDP_GLOBAL_SERVER_PORT 3810; both happy path and STRESS_TEST_CLOSED rejection covered). (c) GlobalTicketRequest 0x006E → GlobalTicket 0x006F (via SendAvatarLogin; failure path tested — galaxy-full timeout returns response_code=1002, exercises UDP_Global.cpp's wire-size fix). (d) GlobalDeleteCharacter 0x0071 → GlobalAvatarList refresh (via SendDeleteCharacter UDP round-trip; landed alongside PacketMethods.h ExtractLong cast bugfix). (e) GlobalCreateCharacter 0x0072 → GlobalAvatarList refresh (via SendCreateCharacter UDP round-trip; landed alongside ColorInfo/ShipData/GlobalCreateCharacter long→int32_t migration AND the GlobalAvatarListCodec AvatarData offset fix AND the sql_query AppendValue UB fix). Only **ProcessGlobalTicket** carries forward — it's the inbound-from-server side that drives 0x006F handoff post-AvatarLogin; the integration test today exercises only the failure branch where the timeout never produces a ticket. The Linux dispatcher lives in `proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp`; unimplemented opcodes still log + return for visibility into what real clients send.

- [~] Port `ProcessSectorServerOpcode` handlers, same staging.
      Status: Wave 3 (2026-05-24, commit bd6f218) lit up the remaining 5 sector opcodes — Linux dispatch is now parity-complete with Win32 ClientToSectorServer.cpp:15-110. Done by porting `UDPClient::ForwardClientOpcode` out of WIN32-walled `proxy/UDPProxyToClient.cpp:19` into `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp` (5 lines: same m_PlayerID/m_ConnectionActive gate + SendResponse(m_ClientPort,...) body — m_ClientPort already populated by existing HandleMasterJoin → SendMasterLogin → 0x2009 confirm path). 10/10 sector cases now match Win32, including: original 5 stubs (LOGIN/MOVE/STARBASE_ROOM_CHANGE/LOGOFF_REQUEST/SERVER_HANDOFF) which now ALSO forward via the new bottom-of-switch `g_ServerMgr->m_UDPConnection->ForwardClientOpcode(...)` (Win32 forwards them too); plus the 5 new handlers — (f) START_ACK 0x0006 forwards + sends 0x3008 STARBASE_LOGIN_COMPLETE or 0x3004 PLAYER_SHIP_SENT based on SectorID, marks LoginComplete on both UDPClient and UDPConnection. (g) TURN 0x0012 rate-limited 1 per 250ms via m_Turn_Sent. (h) TILT 0x0013 same cadence with m_Tilt_Sent. (i) ACTION 0x002C explicit forward + ProcessAction_Linux helper (all 6 Win32 sub-cases are //commented-out logs, no-op). (j) WARP 0x009B HandleWarp_Linux + m_SectorTCPRequest=false + bottom forward; pre-warp SendPositionIfChanged is WIN32-walled in UDPProxyMVAS.cpp — skipped on Linux as documented carry-over (rubber-band UX nit, not correctness). KillTCPConnection from Win32's PLAYER_SHIP_SENT branch lives in WIN32-walled UDPProxyToClient.cpp — skipped; equivalent close happens naturally as server tears state. Default case also forwards (Win32 line 108): server is authority on opcode-meaningful-in-state, proxy just relays.

- [~] Port the UDP proxy plane (`UDPClient`, `UDPProxyMVAS`, `UDPProxyToClient`, `UDPProxyToGlobal`). POSIX UDP sockets + blocking recv thread.
      Status: minimum-viable subset done — HandleMasterJoin now drives a real SendMasterLogin -> server:3808 UDP exchange.
      Touches: `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp` (new, ~390 LOC; POSIX socket + bind to INADDR_ANY:0 ephemeral + connect to game-server:3808, pthread-launched blocking recv thread, full subset for SendMasterLogin + MasterLoginConfirm + WaitForResponse + FixedClientComm; getenv("NET7_GAME_SERVER_HOST") default "server" via getaddrinfo, no hardcoded IP), `proxy/ClientToMasterServer.cpp` (Linux HandleMasterJoin now mirrors the Win32 path: SendMasterLogin -> set port/ip/sector -> SendServerRedirect; falls back to the Phase J option-b local ServerRedirect on -1 timeout), `proxy/Net7.cpp` (Linux main constructs `UDPClient(MVAS_LOGIN_PORT, FIXED, ip_internal)` and registers it as BOTH m_UDPConnection and m_UDPClient — Win32 used two distinct FIXED+MULTI instances; on Linux we only port FIXED so one object serves both roles).
      Notes: docker logs show "UDPClient: resolved game server 'server' -> 172.18.0.6 / UDPClient: bound UDP <ephem> (src) -> 172.18.0.6:3808". Live integration test `MasterJoin.LiveMasterJoinReturnsServerRedirect` goes green; `just integration-test` 6/6. SendMasterLogin currently times out after ~5s (server-side HandleMasterHandoff is "Unimplemented opcode" stub — see server/src/ClientToGlobalServer.cpp:488) and falls through to the local ServerRedirect; the proxy wiring is correct end-to-end. Still WIN32-walled on Linux (not on the MasterJoin->ServerRedirect path; portable independently as their opcode handlers land): position-update thread (`UDPProxyMVAS::MVASThread`), avatar/ticket/global-error UDP path (`UDPProxyToGlobal.cpp` minus the SendMasterLogin slice that lives in UDPClient_linux.cpp), keep-alive / packet-sequence resend, OpenMultiPort / CLIENT_TYPE_MULTI_PORT (client-only).

- [x] Wave 6 (2026-05-24): server→client UDP fan-out (`UDPProxyToClient.cpp` Linux port).
      Status: done — `proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp` (new, ~370 LOC) implements the master-plane recv side that was missing from Wave 3: `ProcessClientOpcode` (direct passthrough to `m_SectorConnection->SendResponse`), `IncommingOpcodePreProcessing` (LOGOFF / SERVER_HANDOFF / START state transitions), `SendClientDataFile` (0x2010 — forward verbatim except 0x0097 galaxy map which is logged), `SendPacketSequence` + `SendLoginPacketSequence` (full reliable-delivery + split-packet reassembly, mirrors Win32 UDPProxyToClient.cpp:138-351 line-for-line with usleep/check_memory swaps), `SendClientPacketSequence` (walks inner [size,opcode,data] tuples; dispatches one TCP frame per opcode via SendResponse since the WIN32-only Queue*/SendQueuedPacket batchers don't exist on Linux), `HandleCustomOpcode` (minimal — 0x2020 stage confirm + 0x100A terminate; launcher-side opcodes 0x2011-0x2019 return false to let the outer raw-forward path fire), `HandleStageConfirm` (sends 0x2021 ACK back to server), `SendCommsAlive`, `RecordLastHandoff`, `KillTCPConnection` no-op. RecvThread switch in `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp` extended with the four new dispatches (0x2010 / 0x2016 / 0x201A / default → ProcessClientOpcode). `proxy/Connection.cpp` Linux SendResponse now takes `m_Mutex` around m_CryptOut + m_SendBuffer + SendAll because the UDP RecvThread is now a concurrent producer with the TCP RecvThread. File-scope `bool g_ShuttingDown = false;` and `uint32_t time_debug = 0;` defined in the new file (Win32 origins are WIN32-walled). What this does NOT include: SendDataFileToClient cache path (depends on WIN32-walled ClientToSectorServer.cpp::SendDataFile) — galaxy-map cache request is logged-no-op; launcher Queue* helpers — opcodes 0x2011/0x2012/0x2013/0x2014/0x2018/0x2019 are forwarded raw rather than batched.
      Touches: `proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp` (new file), `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp` (RecvThread switch + file-comment update), `proxy/Connection.cpp` (Mutex around Linux SendResponse + comment update).
      Notes: clean build, cli-integration 38/38 green from existing stack. Wave 6 unblocks Task #69 (sector LOGIN 0x0002 → START round-trip) — server can now reply to a sector-port client and the proxy will deliver the reply over TCP.

- [!] Wire ticket handoff: bind SSL_LOCALCERT_LOGIN_PORT (3807) on the game server, validate ticket against the same store that net7ssl issued from. Decide: shared MySQL `tickets` table vs. AF_UNIX SOCK_DGRAM push from login to server.
      Status: blocked / re-scoped after Win32 design discovery — there's no cross-process ticket state to share. The Win32 `AccountManager::GetUsernameFromTicket` (login-server/Net7SSL/AccountManager.cpp:839) and the server-side equivalent (`server/src/AccountManager.cpp`) both validate a ticket by `strchr(ticket, '-')` and taking the prefix as the username. The format is `<username>-<rand()>`, with no signature, MAC, or shared secret. "Validation" is effectively a username extraction; anyone who knows a valid username can construct a ticket the server will accept. SSL_LOCALCERT_LOGIN_PORT 3807 is the *other* direction (game server registers itself with login, not ticket transfer) — see Win32 `SSL_Connection.cpp:628 RegisterSectorServer(m_IpAddress, port_number, num_sectors, username)` which adds the sector server to the login service's known-sectors list for ServerRedirect routing decisions.
      Reason for `[!]`: closing as "blocked" because the original framing was wrong; building real ticket sharing now would diverge from Win32 parity. Real cryptographic ticket validation belongs in a future security-hardening Phase, not Phase K. The minimum-viable port already does the right thing — login issues `<user>-<rand>`, proxy/server parses with `strtok(ticket, "-")` and trusts it.
      Touches done (parity): `login-server/Net7SSL/LinuxAuth.cpp:82 BuildTicketLocked` (issues `<user>-<rand>`), `proxy/ClientToGlobalServer.cpp:134` (Win32 path: `strtok(ticket, "-")`).
      Touches remaining (parity): port the `strtok` line into the Linux HandleGlobalConnect when that handler is implemented (carries with `ProcessGlobalServerOpcode` item).
      Open follow-up (out of scope for Phase K): replace string-format ticket with HMAC-signed token, per RFC 6750-style bearer; shared MySQL or Redis ticket store; cap-share between processes via 60-second TTL.

      Touches: `tests/client/master_join_test.cpp`, `tests/CMakeLists.txt`.
      Notes: Verified pass against the running proxy container — `[ OK ] MasterJoin.LiveMasterJoinReturnsServerRedirect` + proxy logs `<client> MasterJoin avatar_id=0 ToSectorID=0 FromSectorID=0`. Probes for `port=3500` at offsets {8, 20} to handle both Win32 and Linux ServerRedirect layouts (see PacketStructures.h follow-up below).
