This complements `master_join_test` (which uses synthesized frames) by replaying actual recorded Win32 client bytes through the live RC4 stream.
      Status: done — LivePostHandshakeReplay went from "send didn't error" to validating that captured Win32 MasterJoin produces a 0x0036 ServerRedirect under live RC4. 8/8 integration tests green; test time +5s as it now blocks on the response.
      Touches: `tests/client/replay_test.cpp`.

- [x] Wire `master_join_test` into the `just integration-test` target.
      Status: done — added `master_join_test` to the cmake `--target` list and `MasterJoin` to the ctest `-R` filter. `just integration-test` now reports 6/6 green (5 prior tests + new MasterJoin round-trip).
      Touches: `justfile`

- [x] PacketStructures.h `long` → `int32_t` migration. The structs sent over the wire use `long`, which is 4 bytes on Win32 and 8 bytes on Linux x86_64. This makes MasterJoin 64 vs 108 bytes and ServerRedirect 10 vs 18 bytes. Half the bug also lived in `proxy/Connection.cpp` Linux SendResponse, which stamped `size = payload + sizeof(long)` (= 8) leaving a 4-byte gap between header and payload.
      Status: in progress — wire structs on the Linux dispatch + UDP master plane migrated. (a) Linux SendResponse `sizeof(long)` → `sizeof(EnbTcpHeader)`. (b) `ServerRedirect` 18B→10B (`port` now at offset 8 on every platform). (c) `VersionRequest` 16B→8B — was working by coincidence (test sends 8B payload; Linux struct's 8B `long` fields aliased the wire bytes thanks to LE truncation in `(uint32_t) request->Major` plus zero-padding for `Minor`). (d) `MasterJoin` 108B→64B — this was the real win. Before: HandleMasterJoin read `avatar_id_lsb`/`ToSectorID` from offsets 32/40 (8-byte field layout), saw zeros, hit the `SendMasterLogin timed-out` fallback path. After: log line now reads `avatar_id=1 ToSectorID=1` from the synthesized test, and `avatar_id=4150549696 ToSectorID=45151` from the captured Win32-client replay — actual captured values, finally parsed correctly. (e) `EnbUdpHeader` 20B→12B + ATTRIB_PACKED — `player_id` + `packet_sequence` were both `long`, so every UDP frame between proxy and server carried an 8-byte gap that a real Win32 client never produces. Server and proxy recompile from the same header so the size flip is symmetric on the Linux-only path. Remaining: other wire structs not yet exercised by the Linux dispatch (GlobalAvatarList, ServerHandoff, GlobalCreateCharacter, Login, et al.) and per-handler memcpy targets.
      Touches done: `proxy/Connection.cpp` (Linux SendResponse), `common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h` — **complete migration**, every `long`/`unsigned long` wire-struct field declaration converted to `int32_t`/`uint32_t`; per-struct comments document why; structs covered in order of when they landed: (a) early 2026-05-24 wave: ServerRedirect, VersionRequest, MasterJoin, EnbTcpHeader, EnbUdpHeader; (b) GlobalAvatarList family — AvatarData/AvatarInfo/Galaxy/GlobalAvatarList::num_galaxies — restored 2042B canonical wire size from 2518B Linux x86_64; (c) ColorInfo / ShipData / GlobalCreateCharacter — restored 17B/207B/539B canonical wire sizes from 21B/226B/571B; (d) commit a82459d sweep (this session): WarpPacket, InvMove, InvSort, ItemState, ChangeBaseAsset, Create, ServerParameters, Login, Navigation, CreateAttachment, DecalItem, Decal, NameDecal, ColorizationItem, Colorization, AvatarDescription, Subparts, ConstantPositionalUpdate, FormationPositionalUpdate, RequestTarget, SetInterface, SetTarget, ActionPacket, ActionPacket2, ClientSetTime, VerbRequest, CameraControl, LogoffRequest, TriggerEmote, NotifyEmote, OptionPacket, SelectTalkTree, ChatStream, ClientChat, ClientChatRequest, ClientChatList, ClientChatError, ClientChatEvent, ClientSkillsRequest, StarbaseAvatarChange{,_S2C}, StarbaseRoomChange, StarbaseRequest, Relationship, ObjectEffect, ObjectToObjectEffect, InitRenderState, ActivateRenderState, SimplePositionalUpdate, PlanetPositionalUpdate, ComponentPositionalUpdate, AdvancedPositionalUpdate, EquipUse, AbilityUse, StarbaseSet, ShipInfo, CTARequest, MovePacket, SkillAction, SkillUse, MissionDismissal, MVASHandoff, ManufactureData, ManufactureTechLevelFilter, FindMember (+ nested fm_item), RecustomizeAvatarStart, RecustomizeShipStart, RecustomizeShipDone, RecustomizeAvatarDone. Knock-on fix: `Player::SetupWarpNavs(short, long *)` decl/defn updated to take `int32_t *target_id` to match WarpPacket.TargetID's migrated type. Also: `server/src/UDP_Master.cpp` (related wire bugs: `sector_id` was over-read 8B for a 4B field; termination packet sent `sizeof(long)=8` where wire expects 4); `server/src/UDP_Global.cpp` (HandleGlobalTicketRequest cast fixes; AVATARLOGIN_CONFIRM reply size 8B→4B); `tests/client/master_join_test.cpp` (probe simplified to {8}).
      Notes: post-Phase-R there is only one PacketStructures.h (under common/) — sibling copies under server/src/ and login-server/Net7SSL/ no longer exist. Post-sweep: server + proxy + login-server build clean; `just cli-integration` 38/38 green from a fresh `docker compose down -v` rebuild. The sweep does NOT cover raw byte-buffer `*((long *) &data[N])` reads in `server/src/SaveManager.cpp` and elsewhere — those are the same class of Linux-port bug (8B reads from 4B wire slots) but live outside PacketStructures.h; tracked as a separate follow-up since they're more invasive (each call site needs the slot offset and surrounding reads verified against the wire frame).

- [x] Per-connection threading in net7ssl (Linux) — detached worker thread per accepted SSL session so a slow handshake doesn't head-of-line block other connects.
      Status: done — `SSL_Listener::HandleAcceptedConnection` runs SSL_accept + read + dispatch + write + close on a detached `std::thread`. Capped at 64 concurrent workers (std::atomic counter); over-cap connections are refused with a log line so a flood can't fork-bomb. Lighter than the Win32 `SSL_Connection` model (no connection pool, no persistent thread per client) but matches the request shape — each TLS session is one round trip.
      Touches: `login-server/Net7SSL/SSL_Listener.h` (+13 LOC: atomic counter, HandleAcceptedConnection decl, <atomic> include), `login-server/Net7SSL/SSL_Listener.cpp` (-50/+65 LOC: inline accept body moves into HandleAcceptedConnection, listener launches detached thread).
      Notes: Verified end-to-end — fired 5 concurrent `curl https://.../AuthLogin` and the proxy log shows 5 interleaved "Accepted SSL connection" / "SSL handshake OK" / "HTTP: GET /AuthLogin" lines instead of strict serial ordering. `just integration-test` 6/6 still green.

- [x] Proxy↔server **global UDP control plane** (UDP_GLOBAL_SERVER_PORT 3810) — wire endpoint for the avatar-list handoff that the kyp-era TCP cluster (SSL_LOCALCERT_LOGIN_PORT 3807) used to carry before Phase Q deleted it. Round-trip path: client sends 0x006D GlobalConnect on TCP 3805 → proxy `UDPClient(global).SendTicket` writes `[u32 0x2002 TICKET]` to server:3810 → server `UDP_Connection::HandleGlobalOpcode` (server/src/UDP_Global.cpp:43) routes through `ProcessTicketInfo` → reads account from net7_user (status, ticket username via strtok) → constructs Player → `SendAvatarList` writes `[u32 0x2003 AVATARLIST]` back to proxy:UDP → proxy forwards as 0x0070 GLOBAL_AVATAR_LIST on TCP 3805.
      Status: done — cli-integration covers both branches of the plane: `GlobalConnectTests.ValidTicket_RoundTripsThroughUdpGlobalPlane_ReturnsAvatarList` (happy path → 0x0070 GlobalAvatarList) AND `GlobalConnectTests.StressTestClosedAccount_GlobalConnect_ReturnsGlobalErrorCode12` (error path → 0x0075 GlobalError code 12 STRESS_TEST_CLOSED). The error path uses an out-of-pool seed account `cli_test_status0` (id 9000010, status=0) so the happy-path Pool stays clean. 35/35 cli-integration green.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/GlobalConnectTests.cs` (sends 0x006D with `[u32 ticket_len_be][char ticket[len]][NUL]` payload; happy-path test waits for 0x0070 and fails loud with the G_ERROR code if 0x0075 arrives instead; error-path test does the inverse — waits for 0x0075, fails loud if 0x0070 arrives since that would mean someone weakened the server's status check), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (+3 entries for 0x006D + 0x0070 + 0x0075, `MinTestedCount` 4→7), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (`StressTestClosed` accessor out-of-Pool), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Smoke/HarnessSmokeTest.cs` (also asserts seed.sql carries the out-of-pool fixture), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (6th account `cli_test_status0`).
      Notes — diagnostics that lit up while landing this:
       - **Seed-account `status` was the blocker.** Server `ProcessTicketInfo` (UDP_Global.cpp:138-143) hard-rejects `status==0` with `G_ERROR_STRESS_TEST_CLOSED` (12). Pool was seeded with status=0 (boilerplate default from the pre-Phase-K era when login was the only path exercised). Real accounts use status=100 (cf. `net7_user.sql` admin row). Fixed in `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` — status now 100 for all five `cli_test0N` accounts plus an enum-table comment.
       - **Proxy's `g_GlobalErrorMsg[]` was truncated.** Table only had codes 0-11; AccountManager.h defines through code 14. When the server returned code 12 the proxy's `Connection::GlobalError` (proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp) logged "unknown error code 12 — dropping" and the client never saw the rejection, masking the seed bug behind a generic timeout. Fixed: full 0-14 table with comments tying each entry to its `G_ERROR_*` constant.
       - **GlobalAvatarList codec landed (third commit, this session).** PacketStructures.h's AvatarData/AvatarInfo/Galaxy/GlobalAvatarList migrated from `long` to `int32_t` to restore Win32 wire-format parity (mirrors the e74f07c MasterJoin migration). Server rebuilt: GlobalAvatarList now serialises to the canonical 2042 bytes (was 2518 on Linux x86_64 with 8-byte longs — silently divergent from Win32). New `GlobalAvatarListCodec` (tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/Inbound/) decodes the full struct: 5 AvatarSlot pairs (AvatarInfo + AvatarData), num_galaxies, and up to 2 Galaxy entries with name/ip/port/player-counts. Happy-path test now asserts `WireSize == 2042`, all 5 slots zero-filled (seeded `avatars` table is empty), galaxy[0].name non-empty, GalaxyID=1, MaxPlayers>0 — these are stronger evidence than "non-empty payload" that the migration took.
       - **The escape-hatch test (`[!]` blocking) is now gone.** The cli-integration Phase T plan can re-open Items 10-12 (enumerate sectors/missions/items workflows) once a GlobalAvatarList codec lands and an avatar can actually be selected → enter sector.
       - **Error-path coverage added (second commit).** `StressTestClosedAccount_GlobalConnect_ReturnsGlobalErrorCode12` validates the inverse path: client → proxy → server (rejects status=0 in `ProcessTicketInfo`) → proxy (forwards via `Connection::GlobalError` + `g_GlobalErrorMsg[12]`) → client. Test asserts both the error code (12) and the human-readable message text from the proxy's table comes through — proves the proxy-table truncation fix landed correctly and would regress loudly if anyone trimmed `g_GlobalErrorMsg[]` back to 12 entries. It also acts as a server-integrity tripwire: if the status-check weakens, the test fails with a CLAUDE.md citation in the failure message.
      Subsystem unblocked: cli-integration now has both the happy-path and the explicit-rejection round-trip locked in. Adding the next UDP-plane scenario (CreateCharacter / DeleteCharacter / SendAvatarLogin) becomes incremental, not architectural.

- [!] `/who.cgi` HTML output — there is nothing meaningful to port here. Win32 reference (`login-server/Net7SSL/SSL_Connection.cpp:256-260`) is a `//TODO` empty branch that leaves `response = nullptr` and falls through. Server-side `m_PlayerMgr.WhoHtml(response_length)` (`server/src/SSL_Connection.cpp:314`) is **referenced but never defined** — there is no `WhoHtml` symbol anywhere in `server/src/`. The Win32 build links because that branch sits inside `#ifdef WIN32` walls that are never compiled (or it's a runtime fault — neither has been verified). Either way, there's no upstream HTML generator to mirror.
      Status: blocked — moved from `[ ]` to `[!]`. The Linux behavior (`MakeNotFound` 404 in `LinuxAuth.cpp:524-526`) is actually *more honest* than the Win32 fall-through, which silently writes nothing and lets the connection die. Building a real implementation belongs in a future phase that also implements `PlayerManager::WhoHtml` from scratch on both platforms — out of scope for "match Phase K parity."
      Touches if revisited: `login-server/Net7SSL/LinuxAuth.cpp` (HTML response writer), `server/src/PlayerManager.cpp` (new WhoHtml method that lists active players), and an IPC channel (shared file / AF_UNIX socket / DB query) since net7ssl can't reach server PlayerManager directly today.

- [x] Wave 7 (2026-05-25): SectorManager constructor uninit `m_JobListCount` segfault — hidden by docker `restart: unless-stopped`.
      Status: done — `server/src/SectorManager.cpp` constructor now initializes `m_JobListCount = 0`. Prior bug: header declares `long m_JobListCount;` with no in-class initializer (SectorManager.h:236); constructor set m_JobTerminalLevel / m_JobListID but not this one. `SetupSectorServer` (SectorManager.cpp:170) only sets it for stations with sector_id>9999 AND JTLevel>0, leaving every non-station sector (and JTLevel==0 stations) iterating garbage in `RefreshJobs` (SectorManager.cpp:1444 — `for (i = 0; i < m_JobListCount; i++) { JobNode *jn = m_JobList[i]; ... }`). RunSectorEventThread calls RefreshJobs first thing (line 787), so every sector pthread crashed on the spot. Crash was invisible because docker-compose `restart: unless-stopped` re-launched the process within ~1s and the container's RestartCount climbed silently while logs looked normal. Diagnosed via dmesg `net7[...]: segfault at 0 ip ... error 4` (user-mode read of non-present page) → coredumpctl stack → addr2line.
      Touches: `server/src/SectorManager.cpp` (+3 lines for m_JobListCount=0 with comment; BeginSectorThread tidied — added pthread_create error log, added bound-port success log, dropped two dead comment lines).
      Notes: After fix, container stays up (RestartCount=0). All 174 sectors log `BeginSectorThread sector_id=N bound UDP port P`. `/proc/net/udp` inside the container shows 197 UDP listeners (174 sector ports + master 3808 + global 3810 + MVASauth 3806 + ephemeral senders). Unblocks Task #69 (sector LOGIN 0x0002 → START round-trip) — was the prerequisite the proxy MVAS plane wiring needed.

- [x] Wave 7 (cont.) — sector LOGIN 0x0002 → START round-trip wiring (2026-05-25, commits d8bfa44 + 7e15637 + 82d955f + ea6d695 + 3ecc04a).
      Status: done — `SectorLoginTests.FullSectorLogin_ReceivesStart` passes in 7s; full 39-test integration suite passes in 2m33s. Approach diverged from the original "third UDPClient on MVAS plane" sketch: instead of standing up a third connect()'d socket, the **global plane was switched to unconnected** (sendto + recvfrom + IP whitelist), because the server-side reality is that **post-login in-game UDP comes from MVASauth (server:3806) to the proxy's global-plane source port** — not a separate destination. The server captures `player->m_Player_Port` from the AVATARLOGIN packet's source port (server/src/UDP_Global.cpp:227) and from then on all `player->m_UDPConnection` writes go to (proxy_ip, that_port) via MVASauth's socket. A connected SOCK_DGRAM on the global plane filtered by single peer port (3810) and silently dropped MVASauth's 3806 frames. Unconnected mode keeps the default-peer for outgoing sendto() (still routes to 3810 for global-control opcodes 0x2003/0x2005/0x200C/0x200E) while accepting recv() from any source port + dropping non-server source IPs.
      Three classes of bug fixed: (a) **proxy SendPacketSequence calling convention** — Linux UDPClient was passing `msg` (payload-only) where Win32 passes the whole UDP packet; SendClientPacketSequence re-casts buffer to EnbUdpHeader and skipped 12 payload bytes, producing the "bad opcode 0x0000 len 0x0" symptom on every 0x2016 frame. Fix: pass `(char *) m_RecvBuffer` for both 0x2016 and 0x201A. (b) **proxy global plane unconnected** — `proxy/Net7.cpp` constructs `udp_to_global` with `unconnected=true`; `proxy/UDPClient.{h,cpp}` ctor takes optional `unconnected` flag stored in `m_Unconnected`; `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp` OpenFixedPort gates connect() on `!m_Unconnected`, UDP_RecvFromServer branches on m_Unconnected to recvfrom + IP whitelist, UDP_Send port==0 branches on m_Unconnected to sendto m_SockAddr. `proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp` activates the global plane on master-handoff confirm and flips LoginComplete on START_ACK so its SendPacketSequence early-return doesn't gate in-game frames. `proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp` HandleStageConfirm forwards exactly 4 bytes (int32_t stage_wire) instead of 8 (sizeof(long)). (c) **sizeof(long) wire-size sweep across server** — `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp` SendOpcode + SendLoginStageConfirm + HandleLoginAckReturn + SendStart + admin Terminate + SendTalkTreeAction + LOOT_HULK_PERMISSION all use int32_t; `server/src/PlayerClass.cpp` SendShipDataToSelf + SendShipData self-branch use int32_t game_id_wire; `server/src/PlayerManager.cpp` force-terminate uses int32_t player_id, and GetPlayer mask widened from `& 0x00FFFFFF` (silently capping at 24 bits) to `~(PLAYER_TAG | MANU_TAG)`; `server/src/UDP_Master.cpp` ProcessHandoff passes int32_t game_id to AddData. `server/src/SectorData.h` IS_PLAYER macro: dropped the bogus `< 0xFFFFF` upper bound that was rejecting every char-based GameID (CharID = account_id*5+slot+1 routinely exceeds 1M; e.g. account 9000001 slot 0 → CharID 45000006). The PLAYER_TAG bit alone is the canonical check, matching the macro's name.
      Bonus fixes that landed alongside: **Phase N MySQL stored-proc inlining** — `accLogin`/`avaLogin`/`avaLogout`/`incWarn`/`logoutOnShutdown` were silent no-ops since libpqxx can't load MySQL DELIMITER blocks; bodies inlined in SaveManager/AccountManager/ServerManager per `db/postgres/seed.sql` source; schema qualifier `net7_user.` stripped from all UPDATEs. **`fflush(stdout)`** after every LogMessage/LogMySQLMsg so `docker compose logs server` sees output in real time (was block-buffered without a TTY → debugging took ~10x longer than necessary). **TlsLoginTests.ValidAccount_ReturnsValidTicket** flaky-assertion fixed (asserted `Length >= 20` against `LinuxAuth.cpp` ticket format `%s-%d` which is 12-21 chars for cli_test01; replaced with `StartsWith(username + "-")` + digit suffix check; comment claimed "40-character hex ticket" the code never emitted).
      Touches: `proxy/Net7.cpp`, `proxy/UDPClient.{h,cpp}`, `proxy/UDPClient_linux.cpp`, `proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp`, `proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp`, `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp`, `server/src/PlayerClass.cpp`, `server/src/PlayerManager.cpp`, `server/src/UDP_Master.cpp`, `server/src/SectorData.h`, `server/src/SaveManager.cpp`, `server/src/AccountManager.cpp`, `server/src/SectorContentSQL.cpp`, `server/src/ServerManager.cpp`, `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/ServerFixture.cs` (WaitForServerSectorAsync polls docker logs for `BeginSectorThread sector_id=10151` marker), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorLoginTests.cs` (new, ~444 LOC), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Handshake/TlsLoginTests.cs` (ticket assertion fix), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0005/+0x2020/+0x2021).
      Notes: end-to-end happy path is now green. Next-Phase-K work is broader opcode coverage (sector enumerate / inventory / movement / chat at sector scope), not handshake plumbing. See decisions log entry 2026-05-25 "Phase K sector LOGIN round-trip GREEN".

- [x] Wave 8 (2026-05-25): first post-START sector opcode — 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT Group/no-group round-trip.
      Status: done — `SectorChatTests.GroupChat_WhenUngrouped_ReceivesNotInGroupErrorString` passes in 7s; full 40-test integration suite passes in 3m6s. Exercises the entire server→client server-side opcode pipeline that every in-sector reply rides: `Player::HandleClientChat` (server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:4544) → `Type==1` branch → `GroupID()==-1` check → `SendVaMessage("Error: You are not in a group!")` → `Player::SendMessageString` (PlayerConnection.cpp:10918) → `SendOpcode(0x001D)` → `m_UDPQueue` → `SendPacketCache` → 0x2016 PACKET_SEQUENCE on UDP → proxy `UDPClient::SendClientPacketSequence` (proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp:531) → 0x001D MESSAGE_STRING on sector TCP 3500. The Group/no-group branch chosen because it is the simplest server-state-independent path that produces a deterministic single-frame reply (slash-commands require GM permissions, broadcast requires another receiver, target/local/guild need extra state; "not in a group" only depends on the default `GroupID()==-1`).
      Refactor that landed alongside: handshake plumbing extracted from `SectorLoginTests.cs` (was ~445 LOC) into reusable `SectorHandshake.cs` static helper (~330 LOC) — `EstablishAsync(server, ticket, account, slot, sectorId, firstName, shipName)` returns a `Session` (IAsyncDisposable with Global+Sector connections + GameId + StartId + Slot); also exposes `DeleteCreatedCharacterAsync` for cleanup. SectorLoginTests is now ~95 LOC orchestration over the helper. Every future post-START opcode test calls `SectorHandshake.EstablishAsync` instead of duplicating the auth → GlobalConnect → CreateCharacter → GlobalTicketRequest → MasterJoin → sector LOGIN → drain-to-START chain.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorChatTests.cs` (new — uses Pool[4] cli_test05 to avoid colliding with SectorLoginTests' Pool[3] and CreateCharacterTests' Pool[2] on IsUsernameUnique), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorHandshake.cs` (new — extracted handshake helper), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorLoginTests.cs` (refactored to use SectorHandshake), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0006 StartAck, +0x001D MessageString), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 15→17; entries for 0x001D MESSAGE_STRING and 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT).
      Notes: no server changes needed — the chat path was wire-correct end-to-end thanks to the Phase K Wave 7 SendOpcode int32_t header fix landing the right wire shape for every server→client opcode. Phase K coverage ratchet: 15/207 → 17/207 (8.2%). Next-Phase-K targets: 0x0044 REQUEST_TIME → 0x0034 CLIENT_SET_TIME (needs HandleRequestTime `*((long *) data)` → `int32_t` fix — same class as the Wave 7 sweep), and 0x0006 START_ACK → 0x0092 CAMERA_CONTROL (closes the login state machine — SetActive(true) gates Active() checks elsewhere).

- [x] Wave 9 (2026-05-25): second post-START sector opcode — 0x0044 REQUEST_TIME → 0x0034 CLIENT_SET_TIME round-trip, with the predicted `sizeof(long)` over-read fix to `Player::HandleRequestTime` **and** a latent `AuxBase::m_Max_Buffer` uninit bug that the test surfaced.
      Status: done — `SectorRequestTimeTests.RequestTime_RoundTripsClientSentTickAndReturnsServerTimes` passes in 7s; full 41-test integration suite green in 3m17s. Exercises the simplest possible in-sector request/reply pair (HandleRequestTime is unconditional — no player-state guards) and asserts that the server echoes the client's exact tick back as `ClientSent`, which is a positive correlation signal: a wire-size or byte-order bug at the server would put garbage in that slot. Client sentinel chosen as `(int)(DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks & 0x7FFFFFFF)` so a leaked-recv-buffer regression can't accidentally echo a matching value run-to-run.
      Server fix 1 — `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:1624` `*((long *) data)` → `*((int32_t *) data)` — same class of bug as the Wave 7 sweep. Win32 wire payload is 4 bytes (a single `long` = 4 on Win32), but the unmigrated Linux server read `*((long *) data)` which is 8 bytes — pulling 4B of payload plus 4B of whatever followed it in the recv buffer, then echoing that garbage back to the client as its own `ClientSent` tick. Test would have failed on the `Assert.Equal(clientTick, echoedClientSent)` line. The `ClientSetTime` struct itself (`common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h:563`) was already migrated to `int32_t` in Wave 7, so the wire size on the reply side (12B) was already correct — only the request-side read was wrong.
      Server fix 2 — `server/src/AuxClasses/AuxBase.cpp` `AuxBase::AuxBase()` now sets `m_Max_Buffer = (unsigned long)-1`. Test surfaced this: SectorRequestTime in isolation passed, but failed in the 3-test sector sequence (SectorLogin → SectorChat → SectorRequestTime) with OperationCanceledException — server log showed thousands of `Error: Bufferoverflow in Aux!` printf bursts and zero `Received RequestTime` entries. Root cause: only 4 of 57 `AuxBase` subclasses (`AuxHulkIndex=1000`, `AuxShipIndex=10000`, `AuxMobIndex=2000`, `AuxHarvestable=2000`) explicitly set `m_Max_Buffer`. The other 53 leave it uninitialised. On Win32 the heap garbage happened to be large enough that `AddData()`'s `sizeof(T)+index < m_Max_Buffer` guard passed through; on Linux the garbage is often small, so every `AddData()` call hits the printf branch. With 3+ players in the same sector, the per-player serialisation flood saturates stdout and stalls the sector tick — REQUEST_TIME never reaches `HandleRequestTime`. The fix initialises `m_Max_Buffer` to `ULONG_MAX` in the base constructor, restoring Win32 de-facto behaviour without weakening any actual bound (the load-bearing safety mechanism is caller-side buffer sizing, not this check; the four subclasses that *know* a tighter cap keep their explicit assignments and override the default).
      Touches: `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp` (HandleRequestTime sizeof(long) fix), `server/src/AuxClasses/AuxBase.cpp` (m_Max_Buffer default), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorRequestTimeTests.cs` (new — uses Pool[5] cli_test06 to avoid colliding with SectorLoginTests' Pool[3] and SectorChatTests' Pool[4]; 90s budget; 200-frame drain cap; xmldoc records all three failure classes including the AuxBase suite-state regression), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (Pool gains cli_test06 entry), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test06 INSERT row; comments updated 9000005→9000006 and "all five"→"all six"), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0034 ClientSetTime, +0x0044 RequestTime), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 17→19; entries for 0x0034 CLIENT_SET_TIME and 0x0044 REQUEST_TIME).
      Notes: Phase K coverage ratchet: 17/207 → 19/207 (9.2%). The HarnessSmokeTest `SeedSql_IsCopiedToOutput_AndMentionsEveryPooledAccount` iterates Pool dynamically so the new 6th account is picked up without test code changes. Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity rules: the `m_Max_Buffer` fix is preservation-aligned (restores 15+ years of upstream behaviour by initialising what was uninitialised — Win32 happened to have large garbage there) and does not loosen any real bound. Next-Phase-K targets: 0x0006 START_ACK closure (SetActive(true) gates Active() checks for movement/inventory), 0x0012 TURN and 0x0013 TILT (simplest movement opcodes — fan-out across sector players is the new path), 0x002C ACTION (combat trigger).

- [x] Wave 17 (2026-05-25): 0x0057 SKILL_UP survival round-trip — untrained skill for the avatar's profession trips the "already maxed" early return (Level=0 == MaxSkillLevel=0).
      Status: done — `SectorSkillUpTests.SkillUp_OnUntrainedSkill_DoesNotBreakConnection_RequestTimeStillRoundTrips` passes; full integration suite green from the running stack. Zero-server-change wave — Phase R already had SkillAction canonical as `{int32_t GameID; int SkillPoints; short SkillID;}` = 10B (common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h:987; `int` is 4B on both Win32 and Linux x86_64 so the struct width is invariant across platforms). SKILL_UP (0x0057) is not explicitly listed in proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp so it hits the `default:` arm at line 514 and falls through to bottom-of-switch `g_ServerMgr->m_UDPConnection->ForwardClientOpcode(...)`. Server-side `Player::HandleSkillAction` (server/src/PlayerSkills.cpp:97) has three early-return guards before any state mutation: (1) `SkillLevel == SkillMaxLevel` (already maxed); (2) insufficient skill points; (3) `SkillLevelRequirement > 0` (prereq skill not high enough). For a SkillID valid in the AuxSkills wrapper but untrained for the avatar's class, the first guard trips on `0 == 0` and the handler returns silently — no DB write, no AuxPlayer/AuxShip refresh, no reply. Pipe survival is the only assertable post-condition (matches Waves 10/11/13/14/15/16 pattern).
      SkillID choice — **and the trap we walked into**: initial draft sent SkillID=169 reasoning from `_Skills::Skill[170]` (server/src/AuxClasses/AuxSkills.h:23-26, the raw data array). **This crashed the sector thread** because the dispatcher path indexes through `m_PlayerIndex.RPGInfo.Skills.Skill[Action->SkillID]` where `RPGInfo.Skills` is `class AuxSkills` (AuxRPGInfo.h:134), whose `Skill` member is **`AuxSkill Skill[64]`** (AuxSkills.h:86) — the wrapper array is only 64 entries, not 170. `AuxSkills::Init` confirms it only loops `i<64` (AuxSkills.h:64-67). Any SkillID ≥ 64 dereferences past the wrapper array end into Player-object memory, reads a garbage `_Skill * Data` pointer, and `GetAvailability()` faults the sector thread (verified via `docker inspect enb-emulator-server-1 --format '{{.RestartCount}}'` = 2 with StartedAt jumping to the same second the bad SKILL_UP frame was sent). Corrected SkillID to **29 SKILL_JENQUAI_CULTURE** — per the seeded Postgres `skills` table, `warrior_max_level = -1` for that row, so the per-profession init loop in PlayerSaves::LoadPlayer (server/src/PlayerSaves.cpp:609-647) skips the entry (`Skills[29].ClassType[0].MaxLevel` stays ≤ 0) and `RPGInfo.Skills.Skill[29]` keeps the AuxSkill::Init defaults (Level=0, MaxSkillLevel=0, Availability[3]=0). First early-return fires, pipe survives.
      Regression coverage — if anyone reverts SkillAction's `int SkillPoints` to `long SkillPoints`, the struct grows from 10B to 14B on Linux x86_64: SkillID would read from byte 12 (instead of 8), past the end of the 10B wire payload, into undefined memory. A garbage SkillID < 64 would index a random AuxSkill wrapper slot — could trip into the mutation path on the wrong skill, corrupting player state. A garbage SkillID ≥ 64 would crash the sector thread on the OOB Data-pointer deref (the same crash that bit the initial test). Also catches: proxy default-case ForwardClientOpcode regression (drops the opcode); m_Mutex deadlock if a regression moves the lock above the early-return guards; dispatcher mis-route at server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:499 (hand-maintained ~200-entry switch); AuxSkills::Init loop-bound shrinking below 30 (the Jenquai-Culture pick at 29 is inside the current `<64` bound, but a regression shrinking it to 32 would crash on Jenquai-Culture too).
      Server-side bounds gap left for future hardening — `PlayerSkills.cpp:101` has no `if (Action->SkillID < 0 || Action->SkillID >= 64) return;` guard before the OOB-prone indexing. The real Win32 client never sends SkillID ≥ 64 (UI only surfaces trainable skills, all in [0..57] per the SkillNames[64] table at PlayerSkills.cpp:37-95), so retail wire never tripped it — it's an existing latent gap, not a regression. Per CLAUDE.md "tightening the server toward greater fidelity is always welcome", a defensive bounds guard would be a legitimate hardening but is intentionally NOT bundled into this wave (scope = exercise the round-trip; the right fix is a `static constexpr int kMaxSkills = 64;` in AuxSkills.h used both for the array decl and the bounds check, and probably part of a broader sweep of similar dispatcher reads). Documented in plans/99-decisions-log.md 2026-05-25 for future contributors.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorSkillUpTests.cs` (new — uses Pool[12] cli_test14 to avoid colliding with Pool[3..11]; 90s budget; 400-frame drain cap; xmldoc documents survival-probe rationale, the AuxSkills::Skill[64] vs _Skills::Skill[170] trap and prior bad reasoning, the SkillID=29 Jenquai-on-Terran-Warrior rationale via skills.warrior_max_level=-1, sizeof(long) SkillAction regression class, proxy default-case fragility, m_Mutex deadlock + dispatcher mis-route + AuxSkills::Init bound hazards, and the CLAUDE.md server-integrity note that the SKILL_UP click is a published retail action), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (Pool gains cli_test14 entry — 9_000_014), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test14 INSERT row, status=100), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0057 SkillUp in sorted position between 0x004E StarbaseRequest and 0x006D GlobalConnect), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 26→27; sorted-position entry for 0x0057 SKILL_UP between 0x004E STARBASE_REQUEST and 0x006D GLOBAL_CONNECT).
      Notes: Phase K coverage ratchet 26/207 → 27/207 (13.0%). Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity rules: zero permissiveness added; the SKILL_UP click on an untrained skill is exactly what the retail Win32 client emits and the retail server's no-op silent-return is faithfully preserved. Next-Phase-K targets: 0x005A VERB_REQUEST (NPC talk-tree exercise — could pair with action=4 STARBASE_REQUEST once we have a seed NPC); 0x0011 NAV (in-flight nav target selection — natural follow-on to MOVE); 0x002F BUY_ITEM (vendor purchase — pure server state mutator on inventory).

- [x] Wave 18 (2026-05-25): 0x005A VERB_REQUEST survival round-trip — SubjectID-mismatch branch trips the silent-no-op early return in `Player::HandleVerbRequest`.
      Status: done — `SectorVerbRequestTests.VerbRequest_OnNonMatchingSubject_DoesNotBreakConnection_RequestTimeStillRoundTrips` passes in isolation (1/1, 7s) AND when the full integration suite is run against a freshly-recycled stack (49/49, 1m32s). Zero-server-change wave — Phase R already had VerbRequest canonical as `{int32_t SubjectID; int32_t ObjectID; int32_t Action;}` = 12B (common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h:570). VERB_REQUEST is not explicitly listed in proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp so it hits the `default:` arm at line 514 and falls through to bottom-of-switch `ForwardClientOpcode(...)`. Server-side `Player::HandleVerbRequest` (server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:3547) is short — reads SubjectID/ObjectID via ntohl(), checks `subject_id == GameID() && pkt->Action == 1`, and on mismatch returns silently. We send SubjectID=0; cli_test15's GameID (assigned by SaveManager as `account_id*5 + slot + 1`) is non-zero, so the equality test fails and the handler exits without entering UpdateVerbs. Pipe survival is the only assertable post-condition (matches Waves 10/11/13/14/15/16/17 pattern).
      Why a survival probe — even on a SubjectID match, `UpdateVerbs(true)` (server/src/PlayerClass.cpp:3500) early-returns when `GetObjectFromID(GetTargetGameID())` returns null (no targeted object), which is exactly the post-handshake state for a freshly-created character. No reply on either branch. Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity rules we don't fabricate one.
      Full-suite re-run gotcha — running SectorVerbRequestTests in isolation first, *then* re-running the full suite (without recycling the docker stack) produced `Failed: 1, Passed: 48` with `G_ERROR_ACCOUNT_IN_USE (code=13)` from cli_test15 at SectorHandshake.cs:89 — the prior isolated invocation left cli_test15 marked active on the global server. Fix: `docker compose down -v && docker compose up -d` between runs cleans the global session state. The full suite green from a fresh stack is the authoritative verification. (Future hardening: the test's `finally` block could explicitly log the account off before deleting the character, instead of relying on session expiry. Out of scope for this wave.)
      Regression coverage — if anyone reverts VerbRequest's three int32_t fields to long, the struct grows from 12B to 16B/20B/24B on Linux x86_64: Action would read from byte 16 (instead of 8), past the end of the 12B wire payload into undefined memory, mis-comparing against the `Action == 1` guard at minimum. SubjectID=0 is a fixed point under byte-swap so ntohl regressions on SubjectID itself wouldn't surface here — but a regression that dropped ntohl on a non-zero SubjectID would suddenly hit (or miss) the GameID-equality branch on the wrong endianness. Also catches: proxy default-case ForwardClientOpcode regression (drops the opcode); dispatcher mis-route at server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:507 (hand-maintained ~200-entry switch); null-deref regression in the equality short-circuit (current code reads both subject_id and object_id from the wire before the equality check).
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorVerbRequestTests.cs` (new — uses Pool[13] cli_test15 to avoid colliding with Pool[3..12]; 90s budget; 400-frame drain cap; xmldoc documents survival-probe rationale, why SubjectID=0 trips the silent-no-op branch, sizeof(long) VerbRequest regression class, proxy default-case fragility, ntohl byte-order regression class, dispatcher mis-route hazard, and the CLAUDE.md server-integrity note that VerbRequest with mismatched SubjectID is exactly what the retail Win32 client would emit if its UI dispatched a verb-refresh outside a targeted context), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (Pool gains cli_test15 entry — 9_000_015), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test15 INSERT row, status=100), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x005A VerbRequest in sorted position between 0x0057 SkillUp and 0x006D GlobalConnect), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 27→28; sorted-position entry for 0x005A VERB_REQUEST between 0x0057 SKILL_UP and 0x006D GLOBAL_CONNECT).
      Notes: Phase K coverage ratchet 27/207 → 28/207 (13.5%). Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity rules: zero permissiveness added; the VerbRequest wire shape is byte-identical to what the retail Win32 client emits on a right-click verb-refresh; the silent-no-op-on-mismatch branch is faithful retail behaviour. Next-Phase-K targets: 0x0011 NAV (in-flight nav target selection — natural follow-on to MOVE); 0x002F BUY_ITEM (vendor purchase — pure server state mutator on inventory); 0x0017 STOP_MOVE or similar movement-state-flip opcodes (Wave 14 MOVE engine-on already established the path).

- [x] Wave 19 (2026-05-25): 0x0017 REQUEST_TARGET direct-reply round-trip — null-target request reaches `Player::HandleRequestTarget` which unconditionally emits 0x0019 SET_TARGET with the hard-coded `-1` sentinel TargetID. **First direct-reply correlation in Phase K post-Wave-9** (Waves 10/11/13/14/15/16/17/18 were all survival probes).
      Status: done — `SectorRequestTargetTests.RequestTarget_OnNullTarget_ReceivesSetTargetWithSentinelTargetIdMinusOne` passes in isolation (1/1, 7s) AND when the full integration suite is run against a freshly-recycled stack (50/50, 2m25s). Zero-server-change wave — Phase R already had RequestTarget canonical as `{int32_t GameID; int32_t TargetID;}` = 8B (common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h:528) and SetTarget canonical with the same shape (PacketStructures.h:540). REQUEST_TARGET is not explicitly listed in proxy/ClientToServer_linux_stubs.cpp so it hits the `default:` arm at line 514 and falls through to bottom-of-switch `ForwardClientOpcode(...)`. Server-side `Player::HandleRequestTarget` (server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:3390) **unconditionally** calls `SendSetTarget(request->TargetID, -1)` regardless of whether the requested target exists — `GetObjectFromID(0)` returns null for both newtarget and oldtarget, `m_ProspectWindow` is false on a fresh starbase character so the OpenInterface branch is skipped, SendSetTarget fires, then `BlankVerbs()` (PlayerClass.cpp:3467 = safe memset) runs and the threat-rank logic at the bottom is gated on `newtarget != null` so it's skipped. This is the **first post-Wave-9 direct-reply opcode** — Waves 10/11/13/14/15/16/17/18 were all survival probes because their handlers either no-op'd, fanned out only to OTHER players, or guarded on player state we couldn't satisfy.
      Why a direct-reply assertion beats a survival probe — survival probes ("pipe still alive") prove the handler didn't crash but say nothing about correctness. Direct-reply with a known sentinel (`-1`) **plus** a known mirror (`GameID == request->TargetID == 0`) **plus** a known payload size (8B) gives three positive-correlation signals in one test. A regression that swapped the SetTarget GameID/TargetID field order would silently break (positive: replyTargetID=-1 would appear in the GameID slot). A regression that widened either field to long would balloon the reply to 12B or 16B (positive: payload length assertion fails immediately). A regression in the server→client UDP fan-out path that affected only this opcode on a sub-branch of `SendClientPacketSequence` framing logic (proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp:531) would surface as a timeout here even though Wave 8's MESSAGE_STRING still passed.
      Full-suite re-run gotcha recurrence — same ACCOUNT_IN_USE-on-re-run pattern as Wave 18: running SectorRequestTargetTests in isolation, then re-running the full suite without recycling the docker stack produced `G_ERROR_ACCOUNT_IN_USE (code=13)` for cli_test16. Fix: `docker compose down -v && docker compose up -d` between runs. This is now the **second wave** to hit this pattern, suggesting a wave-N+1 hardening opportunity: the test's `finally` block could explicitly send `0x0003 LOGOFF` (or whatever the global plane logoff sequence is) before deleting the character, instead of relying on session expiry. Filed as future hardening; out of scope for Wave 19.
      Regression coverage — if anyone reverts RequestTarget's `int32_t GameID/TargetID` to long, the struct grows from 8B to 12B/16B on Linux x86_64: TargetID would read from byte 8 (past the end of the 8B wire payload) into undefined memory, then **walk the threat-rank path on a stale newtarget pointer** which could crash. If SetTarget reply is widened to long, the assertion that payload length is exactly 8 catches it immediately. If the SetTarget struct fields are reordered (GameID/TargetID swap), our assert `replyTargetID == -1` fails (the literal -1 would appear in the GameID slot instead). Also catches: proxy default-case ForwardClientOpcode regression (drops the opcode → timeout); server→client UDP fan-out regression in SendClientPacketSequence framing on a sub-branch unique to this opcode (timeout); dispatcher mis-route at server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:443 (hand-maintained ~200-entry switch); BlankVerbs/SetTargetGameID introducing a crash hazard.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorRequestTargetTests.cs` (new — uses Pool[14] cli_test16 to avoid colliding with Pool[3..13]; 90s budget; 400-frame drain cap; xmldoc documents direct-reply rationale, full HandleRequestTarget code path walk-through, GameID/TargetID mirror semantics, 6 regression classes with primary-source citations, and the CLAUDE.md server-integrity note that TargetID=0 is exactly what the retail Win32 client emits when the user clicks empty space to clear the active target, and the unconditional SetTarget(-1) reply is the faithful retail behaviour — the -1 sentinel means "no rank/threat info yet"), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (Pool gains cli_test16 entry — 9_000_016), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test16 INSERT row, status=100), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0017 RequestTarget and +0x0019 SetTarget in sorted position between 0x0014 Move and 0x001D MessageString), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 28→30; sorted-position entries for 0x0017 REQUEST_TARGET and 0x0019 SET_TARGET between 0x0014 MOVE and 0x001D MESSAGE_STRING).
      Notes: Phase K coverage ratchet 28/207 → 30/207 (14.5%) — first **+2** ratchet in Phase K (both the request and the reply get coverage entries). Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity rules: zero permissiveness added; the REQUEST_TARGET-with-TargetID=0 wire shape is byte-identical to what the retail Win32 client emits when the user clicks empty space; the unconditional SetTarget reply with TargetID=-1 is the faithful retail behaviour. Next-Phase-K targets: 0x0018 REQUEST_TARGETS_TARGET (natural follow-on — the -1 sentinel in our SetTarget reply tells the client to re-request via this opcode, so a 0x0017→0x0018 chain test would exercise both halves of the target-info handshake); 0x002D ACTION2 (simpler-payload sibling of Wave 13's ACTION); 0x0027 INVENTORY_MOVE (first inventory-state-mutator wave); 0x0034 inverse sweep for further opcodes that have no direct reply.

- [x] Wave 53 (2026-05-27): 0x0042 SERVER_PARAMETERS (`SectorManager::SectorLogin2` SPACE-sector emit) **passive-observation +1 ratchet** via Wave-52's 2-stage login pattern. +1 ratchet 84/207 → **85/207 (41.1%)**.
      Status: done — `SectorHandshakeFanoutTests.HandshakeEmitsServerParametersOnSpaceSectorLogin` written; build green; test passes 1/1 in 11s; full 4-test fanout + 6-test coverage suite green.
      Pattern — captures an opcode that ONLY emits on the SPACE-sector branch of the stage-2 sector-login dispatch. `SectorManager::HandleSectorLogin2` at `server/src/SectorManager.cpp:295-304` mirrors Wave-52's `HandleSectorLogin` split: `m_SectorID > 9999` routes to `StationLogin2` at line 299, else to `SectorLogin2` at line 303. `SectorManager::SectorLogin2` at `SectorManager.cpp:354-380` calls `SendServerParameters(player)` at line 364, which is defined at `SectorManager.cpp:264-293` — packs the per-sector server_params (4 float speed multipliers, 4 float backdrop scalars, 1 int backdrop base-asset = 36B `ServerParameters` struct from `common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h`) and emits via `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_0042_SERVER_PARAMETERS, ..., sizeof(parameters))` at line 292. `SectorManager::StationLogin2` at `SectorManager.cpp:507-535` (the m_SectorID>9999 branch) calls `SendShipInfo` / `SendMessageString` / `SendStarbaseSet` / `SendLoungeNPC` / `SendStart` but does NOT call `SendServerParameters` — that's why no prior handshake-fanout test using sectorId=10151 ever captured 0x0042.
      Test — new `HandshakeEmitsServerParametersOnSpaceSectorLogin` in `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorHandshakeFanoutTests.cs` mirrors Wave 52's structure: stage 1 `EstablishAsync` at sectorId=10151 creates the avatar via `ReInitializeSavedData` (fresh char, no `avatar_level_info` row yet); explicit `0x00B9 LOGOFF_REQUEST` → `0x00BA LOGOFF_CONFIRMATION` round-trip forces synchronous `DropPlayerFromGalaxy` via `Player::HandleLogoffRequest` at `PlayerConnection.cpp:7709-7720`; stage 2 `ReestablishAsync` at sectorId=1015 reconnects against the existing avatar, `ReadSavedData` takes the `ReloadSavedData` path which preserves the LOGIN packet's sector_num via the `secNum`-save-then-`Reset()`-then-restore at `PlayerSaves.cpp:289-291`, and `HandleSectorLogin2` routes through `SectorLogin2`'s `SendServerParameters` emit. Single `Assert.Contains(OpcodeId.Known.ServerParameters.Value, spaceSession.HandshakeOpcodes)` — clean per-wave failure attribution distinct from Wave 52's SectorLogin (stage-1 sub-stage) emits at SectorManager.cpp:344+347.
      Legitimacy — passive-observation only. 0x0042 is a server-originated emit that has always been produced on every space-sector stage-2 handshake; Wave 53 just records ground truth from a dispatch sub-stage the suite hadn't exercised. Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity, no widened input acceptance, no relaxed posture, no fabricated replies, no server changes — exactly the "tightening" the rule welcomes.
      Regression coverage — 7 classes documented inline in test xmldoc: (a) `HandleSectorLogin2` dispatch-branch collapse (m_SectorID>9999 always taking the station path would silently lose 0x0042); (b) `SendServerParameters` removal or guard regression at SectorManager.cpp:364 (current call is unconditional, a conditional gate on `m_SectorData->server_params` validity would break the assertion); (c) `ServerParameters` struct layout regression in `common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h` (currently 36B = 9 × 4B floats/int; a sizeof regression would produce opcode 0x0042 with mangled payload — opcode-level assertion would pass, future typed-codec wave would catch byte-layout drift); (d) `SendOpcode` header-width revert at `PlayerConnection.cpp:127`; (e) proxy `SendClientPacketSequence` inner-opcode guard tightening at `proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp:568` (currently passes 0x0042 < 0x0FFF); (f) SectorLogin2 stage-machine progression regression — if `PlayerManager::ProcessNextLoginStage` stops advancing past HandleSectorLogin into HandleSectorLogin2, the entire SectorLogin2 emit fan (0x0042 plus SendShipInfo's downstream ItemBase/AuxData chain, SendAllNavs, etc.) would be missing — 0x0042 is the cleanest single-emit signal for that stage being reached; (g) DoSectorLoginUntilStartAsync drain-loop capture path.
      Seam-discovery — Wave 53 is the second wave (after Wave 52) to exercise the 2-stage station→space login. Remaining SPACE-only stage-2 emit candidates still on the table: `m_ObjectMgr->SendAllNavs(player)` at SectorManager.cpp:369 (per-nav opcodes via `Object::SendObject` — variable count, opcode depends on `obj->ObjectType()` — needs investigation for OT_PLANET on sector 1015); `SendShipInfo`'s SpeedFactor=1 (space) vs SpeedFactor=2 (station) differentiation at SectorManager.cpp:360-361 (passive observation on the wire frame, not a new opcode); `SendVaMessage("We have entered %s Sector...")` at SectorManager.cpp:377 (already covered as 0x001D via prior MESSAGE_STRING tests); `SendStarbaseSet(0, 0)` at StationLogin2:512 vs nothing on SectorLogin2 (negative observation: 0x004F should NOT appear in space-sector handshakes). Wave 53 lights up the lowest-hanging fruit (the unconditional 36B-frame emit) — Wave 54+ explores SendAllNavs.
      Touches: `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorHandshakeFanoutTests.cs` (new `HandshakeEmitsServerParametersOnSpaceSectorLogin` test with full xmldoc), `tools/cli-client/src/CliClient.Core/Opcodes/OpcodeId.cs` (+0x0042 ServerParameters in sorted position between 0x0040 ConstantPositionalUpdate and 0x0044 RequestTime), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/TestAccounts.cs` (`HandshakeEmitsServerParametersOnSpaceSectorLogin` → cli_test50 9_000_050), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Fixtures/seed.sql` (cli_test50 INSERT row, status=100), `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Coverage/TestedOpcodes.cs` (MinTestedCount 84→**85**; +1 sorted-position entry for 0x0042 SERVER_PARAMETERS between 0x0040 and 0x0044 with full regression-class commentary).
      Notes: Phase K coverage ratchet 84/207 → **85/207 (41.1%)**. Direct-reply/survival-probe pattern count unchanged at 24 waves; passive-observation pattern count now 4 waves (Wave 34, Wave 35, Wave 52, Wave 53 — 29 opcodes total). Stale-state diagnostic: when re-running the full fanout suite immediately after a single-test run, cli_test50 hit `G_ERROR_ACCOUNT_IN_USE` because the in-memory Player from the single run hadn't been released — the test passes cleanly on a fresh stack; this is a known limitation of the 2-stage pattern that future waves (Wave 54+) should address by extending stage-2 disposal with its own LOGOFF_REQUEST round-trip. Next-Phase-K targets after Wave 53: SendAllNavs per-nav fanout (Wave 54 candidate); 0x003A SERVER_HANDOFF via WARP-on-space; upgrade existing 24 survival-probe assertions to byte-exact reply-shape assertions.

- [x] Wave 54 (2026-05-27): 0x003F PLANET_POSITIONAL_UPDATE + 0x0099 NAVIGATION (per-planet SendObject fan-out via `ObjectManager::SendAllNavs`) **passive-observation +2 ratchet** via Wave 52/53's 2-stage login pattern. +2 ratchet 85/207 → **87/207 (42.0%)**.
      Status: done — `SectorHandshakeFanoutTests.HandshakeEmitsPlanetPositionalUpdateAndNavigationOnSpaceSectorLogin` written; build green; test passes; full 5-test fanout + 6-test coverage suite green.
      Pattern — captures the per-planet SendObject emit chain that ONLY fires on the SPACE-sector branch of the stage-2 sector-login dispatch. `SectorManager::SectorLogin2` at `server/src/SectorManager.cpp:369` calls `m_ObjectMgr->SendAllNavs(player)`, which is defined at `server/src/ObjectManager.cpp:406-424`. `SendAllNavs` iterates `m_StaticSectorList`, skipping OT_DECO and inactive entries; for each remaining OT_PLANET (or previously-exposed) object not already in the player's `ObjectRangeList`, it calls `obj->SendObject(player)`. The per-planet SendObject chain emits 0x003F PLANET_POSITIONAL_UPDATE (the orbital positional update — distinct from 0x003E ADVANCED for non-orbital and 0x0040 CONSTANT for static) and 0x0099 NAVIGATION via `Player::SendNavigation` (`PlayerConnection.cpp:1138-1148`, packs {int32 GameID; float Signature; char visited; int32 NavType; char IsHuge} via `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_0099_NAVIGATION, ..., sizeof(navigation))`). `SectorManager::StationLogin2` at `SectorManager.cpp:507-535` does NOT call `SendAllNavs` (stations have no static nav roster — only the manufacturing-lab pseudo-object created in `StationLogin`'s `SendCreate(ManuID, ..., CREATE_MANU_LAB)` at SectorManager.cpp:478), so both opcodes are strictly diagnostic of the SPACE-sector SendAllNavs fan-out.
      Test — new `HandshakeEmitsPlanetPositionalUpdateAndNavigationOnSpaceSectorLogin` in `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorHandshakeFanoutTests.cs` reuses Wave 52/53's 2-stage station→space pattern: stage 1 `EstablishAsync` at sectorId=10151 creates the avatar via `ReInitializeSavedData`; explicit `0x00B9 LOGOFF_REQUEST` → `0x00BA LOGOFF_CONFIRMATION` round-trip forces synchronous `DropPlayerFromGalaxy`; stage 2 `ReestablishAsync` at sectorId=1015 (Luna space) routes through `SectorLogin2`'s `SendAllNavs` call. Two assertions — `Assert.Contains(OpcodeId.Known.PlanetPositionalUpdate.Value, spaceSession.HandshakeOpcodes)` and `Assert.Contains(OpcodeId.Known.Navigation.Value, spaceSession.HandshakeOpcodes)` — both ride the same per-planet SendObject emit site so a regression that takes out one will almost certainly take out the other; bundling keeps the 120s live-stack cost amortised across two coverage rows.
      Legitimacy — passive-observation only. Both opcodes are server-originated emits that have always been produced on every space-sector stage-2 handshake into a planet-bearing sector; Wave 54 just records ground truth from a per-planet emit path the suite hadn't exercised. Per CLAUDE.md server-integrity, no widened input acceptance, no relaxed posture, no fabricated replies, no server changes — exactly the "tightening" the rule welcomes.
      Regression coverage — 8 classes documented inline in test xmldoc and TestedOpcodes commentary: (a) `SendAllNavs` removal or guard regression at SectorManager.cpp:369 (call is currently unconditional; a conditional gate that doesn't fire for sector 1015 would silently lose both opcodes); (b) `SendAllNavs` iterator-skip regression at ObjectManager.cpp:416 (OT_PLANET-or-previously-exposed gate inverted — a fresh player has empty ExposedNavList so OT_PLANET is the always-visible branch); (c) `m_StaticSectorList` load regression — if sector 1015's static-object roster fails to load at sector-thread spawn time, the loop iterates an empty list; (d) `Object::SendObject` vtable regression at ObjectClass.h:365 (the base virtual dispatches per-type to derived classes; a vtable corruption or override-removal that hits the OT_PLANET path would silently produce no PLANET_POSITIONAL_UPDATE/NAVIGATION); (e) `SendNavigation` opcode-id typo at PlayerConnection.cpp:1147 (a flip from `ENB_OPCODE_0099_NAVIGATION` to a neighbouring define would still emit a frame but under a different opcode); (f) `SendOpcode` header-width revert at PlayerConnection.cpp:127; (g) proxy `SendClientPacketSequence` inner-opcode guard tightening at proxy/UDPProxyToClient_linux.cpp:568 (currently passes 0x003F and 0x0099, both < 0x0FFF); (h) DoSectorLoginUntilStartAsync drain-loop capture path.
      Seam-discovery — Wave 54 closes the loop on the SectorLogin2 SPACE-arm fan-out enumeration started in Wave 53 by hitting the `m_ObjectMgr->SendAllNavs(player)` call site Wave 53 explicitly punted as the next investigation target. The 0x003F + 0x0099 pair is the lowest-hanging-fruit subset of the per-planet SendObject chain (OT_PLANET nav-list always-visible); the same SendObject path also emits 0x0004 CREATE, 0x0089 RELATIONSHIP, and 0x001B AUX_DATA, all of which are already covered by Wave 51's `HandshakeEmitsFullSendLoginShipDataFanout` test on a different code path (the player-self SendCreate→SendShipInfo chain) — Wave 54 doesn't re-light them here to avoid double-coverage credit.       Regression coverage — 6 NEW classes Wave 13's survival probe is structurally blind to: (a) ActionPacket struct-layout drift at common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h:546 — if a contributor reverts the Wave 11 `long`→`int32_t` migration, the struct grows from 16B to 32B on Linux x86_64 and the handler reads `Action` from offset 8 (where the wire has Target's high half) instead of offset 4 — Wave 13's case=23 still hits the default arm (silent — survival passes) but Wave 124 fails because Action=26 reads as garbage and never reaches case 26; (b) `ObjectManager::GetObjectFromID(0)` regression at ObjectManager.cpp:567 — the first arm explicitly returns the zero-initialised local for `0 <= id < m_StartObjectID`; a refactor that initialised `obj` to `m_SectorIndexList[0]` "for safety" would make `!obj` false in case 26's body, skip the error branch, fall through to the OT_PLAYER ability-use check — drain-timeout catches; (c) `SendVaMessage`→`SendMessageString` color-default regression at PlayerClass.h:277 — declaration reads `SendMessageString(char *msg, char color=5, bool log=true)`; if the default were lost or changed (e.g. to 17 to match case-1's incapacitation arm) the third byte of the wire body would change — Wave 124 pins `span[2]==5`; (d) `SendMessageString` length-field width regression — the current emit writes a `short` (u16 LE) length; if a refactor promoted the field to `int` (u32 LE) the payload would be 43B not 41B and offset 2 would be 0 instead of color — Wave 124 pins both total length (41) and the u16 at offset 0 (38); (e) the fixed error string drifting — `SendVaMessage("Invalid JS target! SUBMIT BUG REPORT!")` at PlayerConnection.cpp:4151 is a literal; a typo-correction PR that "fixed" punctuation, dropped "SUBMIT BUG REPORT!", or localised the message silently changes body bytes — Wave 124 pins the verbatim 37-byte ASCII run + trailing NUL; (f) case-26 → SendMessageString call-site rewire — a refactor that wrapped the error reply in a `SendClientChatError`-flavour emit (0x00A6 instead of 0x001D) silently changes the response opcode; Wave 124's opcode-filter on MessageString catches the absence.

      Server-integrity (POSITIVE per CLAUDE.md). Case-26 is the retail Jumpstart ability click; the retail client hands in `Target=GameID-of-incapacitated-ally` and the server runs the ability. The "Invalid JS target!" arm is what the retail server emits when the click arrives with no resolvable target — e.g. the targeted player left the sector between selection and click. Wave 124 exercises that POSITIVE-fidelity path by presenting Target=0 (the same shape the retail client sends on a no-selection click) and asserting the retail-shape reply. No server permissiveness added: a real-client click with no target lands on the exact same code path.

Neighbouring case-'a' arms `MatchOptWithParam("altweapon", "altname", "addbaseore", ...)` all reject "authlevel" at the strncmp-then-first-char-after-prefix-character-class probe in `MatchOptWithParam` (PlayerConnection.cpp:4526-4554) since the strings differ at position 1 ('a' vs 'l'/'d'). `AdminLevel()` returns 100 (=`ADMIN`, Net7.h:366) for status=100 seed.sql accounts via the character-create path at `login-server/Net7SSL/AccountManager.cpp:950` (`admin_level = ntohl(GetAccountStatus(username))`). `SendVaMessage` (PlayerClass.cpp:3415-3425) vsprintf_s's the format into "Authentication Level - Num: 100" (31 bytes), forwards to `SendMessageString(pch)` with default `color=5` (PlayerClass.h:277). `SendMessageString` (PlayerConnection.cpp:10987-10997) emits `[u16 LE length=32][u8 color=5][31B ASCII][NUL]` total 35 bytes via `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_001D_MESSAGE_STRING, buffer, length + 3)`. FIFTEENTH byte-exact direct-reply pin. **NINTH multi-arm dispatcher sibling-arm-pinning wave** — after Waves 115/116/118/119/120/121/122. **HandleClientChat dispatcher now has TWO byte-exact-pinned dispatch forks** — Wave 110 pins the Type-keyed `Type==1` no-group arm with the 34-byte "Error: You are not in a group!" reply; Wave 123 pins the type-agnostic String[0]=='/' slash short-circuit with the 35-byte /authlevel reply. The HandleClientChat dispatcher has TWO control-flow tiers: (A) slash-prefix short-circuit `String[0]=='/'` ahead of the Type fanout — Wave 123; (B) Type-keyed fan-out for chat->Type ∈ {0=Target,1=Group,2=Guild,3=Local,4=Broadcast} — Wave 8/110 on Type==1. Sibling-arm-pinning catalogue now spans TEN pairs across FIVE distinct emit functions (SendMessageString via SendVaMessage now has TWO byte-exact-pinned sibling pairs on the SAME emit fn: Wave 110+123 on HandleClientChat's slash vs Type fanout; Waves 117+118 on HandleClientChatRequest's CCR_LIST_CHANNELS+CCR_LIST_ALL_CHANNELS post-switch T2 arms — cross-dispatcher sibling pairs landing on the same SendVaMessage→SendMessageString emit pipeline).
      Regression coverage — 6 NEW classes Waves 8/110 are structurally blind to: (a) slash-prefix dispatch fork at `PlayerConnection.cpp:4614` — Waves 8/110 send non-slash content so the `chat->String[0] == '/'` branch is never taken; a regression that moves the slash check inside one of the Type-keyed arms (e.g. "only honour /-commands on Type=Target") silently drops /authlevel from a Type=Group sender — Wave 110's no-group reply would START firing in its place (different literal, different byte count, but still produces a frame) — Wave 123's literal "Authentication Level" Assert.Contains catches; (b) `HandleSlashCommands` user-block guard regression at `PlayerConnection.cpp:5434` — Waves 8/110 never enter HandleSlashCommands; the user-block guard `(Msg[0] == '/') && (Msg[1] != 0) && (!msg_sent || !success)` has THREE conjunct clauses; a regression that flips the last clause to `(msg_sent && success)` (an "only fall through after a GM-block emit" misreading) would cause the user-block to skip /authlevel from the first attempt — Wave 123 fails immediately on drain-timeout; (c) case-'a' switch arm reorder at `PlayerConnection.cpp:5444-5460` — a regression that reshuffles case labels (e.g. drops case 'a' fall-through after fall-through-to-default in a switch rewrite) routes /authlevel to default, which emits nothing — Wave 123 catches by drain-timeout; (d) strcmp→strncmp regression at `PlayerConnection.cpp:5455` — a tighter `strcmp(pch, "AuthLevel")` (case sensitivity flip) drops the match; Wave 123 fails on literal absence; (e) %d format-specifier width regression in `SendVaMessage` — AdminLevel returns int; a regression to %ld on a platform where long is 8B while a 4B int sits on the va_list would print garbage; Wave 123's verbatim "100" assertion catches; (f) AdminLevel seeding regression at `login-server/Net7SSL/AccountManager.cpp:950` — character-create sets `admin_level = ntohl(GetAccountStatus(username))`; a regression that defaults admin_level to 0 emits "Authentication Level - Num: 0" — Wave 123 catches by literal byte-count mismatch (29 vs 31) AND the verbatim "100" assertion.

The 35-byte 0x001D MESSAGE_STRING reply is exactly what the retail Win32 client's MESSAGE_STRING decoder was compiled to receive. No client stimulus addition beyond the same 0x0033 CLIENT_CHAT Wave 8 already sends with a different body, no server change, no widened input acceptance.

For the 81-byte verbatim literal `"Sorry, the server is not currently accepting new logins.  Please try again later."` (NOTE: double-space after "logins." preserved verbatim from retail): msg_len = 81 LE = bytes 0x51 0x00 0x00 0x00, err+7 = 19 BE = bytes 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x13, total payload = 89. SEVENTH byte-exact upgrade of a direct-reply assertion. **FIRST byte-exact wave on a PROXY-emit path** — six prior byte-exact waves all pinned server-side emit paths (SendMessageString, SendPriorityMessageString, SendClientChatEvent); Wave 114 is the FIRST to pin a proxy-side emit (Connection::GlobalError). **FIRST byte-exact wave on a hybrid LE+BE framing path** — the msg_len field is written in host-byte-order (LE on Linux x86_64) while the error code field is written via ntohl producing BE; this LE/BE interleave is unique to the proxy's GlobalError path. The four-byte cross-asymmetry (LE for one field, BE for the next) catches subtle "consistency cleanup" regressions Wave 114's exact-byte pins document explicitly. Also the FIRST byte-exact wave that doesn't depend on a character/sector handshake — proves the byte-exact methodology generalises to global-plane control-plane responses, not just sector-plane direct-reply emits.

      Status: code landed; **test verified locally — PASSED 7s** (single-test --filter run; full SectorClientChatRequestTests suite also re-verified PASSED 7s+7s — Wave 32 unaffected). firstName "Cher113" picked to satisfy both AccountManager sharp edges — has lowercase vowel 'e' (passes G_ERROR_ONE_VOWEL check at AccountManager.cpp:1147) and no three-in-a-row repeated characters (the "11" peaks at 1 repeat, count never hits 2; passes G_ERROR_REPEATING_CHAR check at AccountManager.cpp:1158-1166).
      **PRESERVATION DISCOVERY** — this wave surfaced two preservation-grade findings that Wave 32's existing docstring assumptions got wrong:
        (1) **Test characters are AdminLevel=ADMIN, not USER.** Seeded test accounts at Fixtures/seed.sql carry `status=100` to satisfy the global UDP plane's ProcessTicketInfo gate (G_ERROR 12 otherwise). At character creation, `AccountManager.cpp:1221` initialises the avatar's admin_level from `GetAccountStatus(account_username)` directly: `database.info.admin_level = ntohl(GetAccountStatus(...))`. Account status 100 matches the ADMIN constant at Net7.h:366 — so every test character is ADMIN, NOT USER. This propagates through `Player::GetPostFix` at PlayerConnection.cpp:10303's `case ADMIN` branch which sets `PostFix="ADMIN"`, then line 10323's `snprintf(FName, length, "%s [%s]", Name(), "ADMIN")` produces `FName="Cher113 [ADMIN]"` (15 bytes) — the LastName emitted on the wire is NEVER the raw firstName for test characters.
        (2) **AddDataLS("") emits short(0), not zero bytes.** The four trailing string parameters of `Player::SendClientChatEvent` default to `""` (empty string literal), NOT NULL — declaration at PlayerClass.h:1031: `void SendClientChatEvent(long Type, Player *Source, char *Channel="", char *Message="", char *OtherPlayer="", char *NonPlayerSrc="")`. AddDataLS at PacketMethods.h:66-74 guards on `if (mydata)` — a non-null pointer to `""` PASSES the guard and emits `short(strlen("")) == short(0)` followed by zero bytes of payload, i.e. 2 bytes per empty-string AddDataLS. The three Channel/Message/OtherPlayer AddDataLS calls emit 6 bytes total — NOT zero as a NULL-pointer reading of the signature would suggest. These findings retroactively correct Wave 32's xmldoc assumption of "USER admin level, no `[XYZ]` postfix" and the inline "AddDataLS NULL emits NOTHING" reading.
      Pattern — frame-count/shape hardening, TWENTY-SECOND in Phase K. SIXTH byte-exact upgrade of a direct-reply assertion. **FIRST byte-exact wave on the `SendClientChatEvent` framing path** — extends the byte-exact catching surface beyond the SendMessageString (Waves 108/109/110/111) and SendPriorityMessageString (Wave 112) paths to the structurally distinct 0x00A5 CLIENT_CHAT_EVENT wire shape: int32 Type + int32 spacer + length-prefixed strings (short-LE length, no NUL) + tail of (short(0), int32(0)). This is the FIRST byte-exact wave to exercise the AddDataLS length-prefixed-string framing pattern, which differs from AddDataSN (Wave 112) and SendMessageString (Waves 108-111) — three distinct framing patterns now byte-exact pinned.
      Regression coverage — 7 NEW classes Wave 32 was structurally blind to (full detail in test xmldoc): (a) AdminLevel-derived PostFix branch mis-selection at PlayerConnection.cpp:10303 — dropping or renaming `case ADMIN` flips LastName from "Cher113 [ADMIN]" to "Cher113" or "Cher113 [ADM]"; (b) AccountManager admin_level-from-status seeding regression at AccountManager.cpp:1221 — breaks the `admin_level = ntohl(GetAccountStatus(...))` seeding path; (c) AddDataLS empty-string emit divergence — a switch from `if (mydata)` to `if (mydata && *mydata)` would skip the short(0) emit and shrink the reply by 6 bytes (54→48); (d) LastName duplicated-emit refactor at PlayerConnection.cpp:10367-10368 — removing the dup shrinks reply by 17 bytes (54→37); (e) spacer/trailing-field drift — int32(0) at offset 4 and short(0)×4 + int32(0) at offsets 42-50 are Wave 32 blind spots; (f) AddData<long> vs AddData<short> width mix-up — interleaved 4B/2B emits cascade offsets on any width regression; (g) variable-length walker request-side regression at PlayerConnection.cpp:1659-1692 — request walker regression that still emits 0x00A5 but with different body shape (e.g. via mis-routed case label) is caught by Wave 113 where Wave 32 is blind.
      Server-integrity POSITIVE — pure passive-observation tightening of an already-driven direct-reply path. Same 18B `CLIENT_CHAT_REQUEST` stimulus (PlayerID=0, type=28 CCR_FRIEND_STATUS_ONLY, three short(0) string-lengths, int32(0) data-size) as Wave 32. No client stimulus addition, no server change, no widened input acceptance, no fabricated reply. The 54-byte response is exactly what the retail Win32 client's CLIENT_CHAT_EVENT decoder was compiled to receive for an ADMIN-status player. The ADMIN postfix on the LastName is a property of the seeded test-account status field (100, set in seed.sql to satisfy ProcessTicketInfo), not server divergence — a retail player whose account status is ADMIN sees exactly this wire shape.

Try later!", "MessageLine", 1000, 4)` → builds `[msg1+'\0'][msg2+'\0'][int32_t time LE][int32_t priority LE]` via `AddDataSN` + `AddData<long>` (template specialisation at `PacketMethods.h:37-42` forces 4-byte emission on Linux x86_64 where sizeof(long)==8) → `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_0020_PRIORITY_MESSAGE, &buffer[0], index)`. FIFTH byte-exact upgrade of a direct-reply assertion. **FIRST byte-exact wave on the `SendPriorityMessageString` framing path** — extends the byte-exact catching surface beyond the SendMessageString-based 0x001D path (Waves 108/109/110/111) to the structurally distinct 0x0020 PRIORITY_MESSAGE wire shape: two AddDataSN-emitted NUL-terminated strings followed by two AddData<long> 4-byte LE ints, no length prefix, no colour byte. Catches a different class of regression: any `AddData<long>` template-specialisation revert (back to plain 8-byte long emit) would push the tail from 8 to 16 bytes and the 71-byte length assertion trips immediately. Conversely, regressions to `SendMessageString` length-field width / color-default that Waves 108/109/110/111 catch would NOT surface here (different code path). The two catching surfaces are fully complementary.

Continuing +0 hardening to pin frame-count invariants on existing tested opcodes (Wave 87 → 104 sibling pattern) preserves real preservation value with bounded scope.
- [x] Wave 103 (2026-05-28): **0x0092 CAMERA_CONTROL +1 ratchet via space-arm START_ACK stimulus — FIRST in-suite assertion against a SendCameraControl emit — coverage 104/207 → 105/207 (50.2% → 50.7%).** Stimulus-driven direct-reply round-trip closing the gap Wave 10's `SectorStartAckTests.cs` explicitly documented as "genuinely unreachable" for a freshly-created character. Wave 103 light it up via the 2-stage station→space pattern: stage 1 `EstablishAsync(10151)` creates the avatar at Luna Station via the `StationLogin` arm (`sector_num=10151 > MAX_SECTOR_ID=9999` so `HandleStartAck` skips SendLoginCamera — same as Wave 10); explicit `0x00B9 LOGOFF_REQUEST` → drain to `0x00BA LOGOFF_CONFIRMATION` synchronously runs `DropPlayerFromGalaxy`; stage 2 `ReestablishAsync(1015)` reconnects (no char create) and LOGINs to Luna space, `Player::ReadSavedData`'s `ReloadSavedData` path at `server/src/PlayerSaves.cpp:289-291` preserves the LOGIN packet's `ToSectorID=1015` so the second handshake terminates at `0x0005 START` with `sector_num=1015 < MAX_SECTOR_ID`. Client then sends `0x0006 START_ACK` with empty payload (`Player::HandleStartAck` at `server/src/PlayerConnection.cpp:1613-1627` takes `unsigned char *data` but never dereferences it — matches retail wire shape). HandleStartAck flips `SetActive(true)` at PlayerConnection.cpp:1617 and dispatches the SPACE arm at line 1623 (`if (PlayerIndex()->GetSectorNum() < MAX_SECTOR_ID)`), calling `SendLoginCamera()` at line 1625. `Player::SendLoginCamera` (`server/src/PlayerClass.cpp:511-531`) — inside the `Active()` guard satisfied by the prior `SetActive(true)` — runs `FindGate(m_FromSectorID)` (null for a fresh login, no gate arrival), falls through the conditional, and unconditionally calls `SendCameraControl(m_CameraSignal, m_CameraID)` at PlayerClass.cpp:528. `Player::SendCameraControl` at PlayerConnection.cpp:4516-4524 emits `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_0092_CAMERA_CONTROL, &data, sizeof(data))` with the 8-byte `CameraControl` struct (two int32_t fields + ATTRIB_PACKED per `common/include/net7/PacketStructures.h`, verified via `tr -d '\r' < PacketStructures.h | grep -A 6 'struct CameraControl'`). Test drains until `0x0092` and asserts `payload.Length == 8`. **+1 ratchet (0x0092 was uncounted before Wave 103); coverage 104/207 → 105/207 (50.2% → 50.7%).**
      Status: code landed; **test verified locally — PASSED 11s** (full run 3.72 min including docker compose stack startup).
      Pattern — direct-reply stimulus-driven ratchet. - [x] Wave 90 (2026-05-28): **0x00B4 SUBPARTS byte-exact 54-byte hardening for Terran-Warrior race/profession — seventeenth hardening-pattern wave, first framing-audit hardening (hand-rolled variable-shape emit).** Pins the byte-exact 54-byte payload shape of every 0x00B4 SUBPARTS frame the server emits during the station-sector login handshake stream for the Terran-Warrior race/profession every TestAccounts seed spawns as (race=0=RACE_TERRAN, profession=0=Warrior — hardcoded in `tests/integration/CliClient.IntegrationTests/Opcodes/SectorHandshake.cs:258-260` via `SectorHandshake.CreateCharacterOnSlotAsync`). 0x00B4 is server-emitted by `Player::SendSubparts` at `server/src/PlayerClass.cpp:988-1043`. Unlike the fixed-shape `sizeof(struct)` opcodes hardened by Waves 67/71/76-89, the SUBPARTS payload is composed dynamically via `AddData<T>` / `AddDataS` helpers from `server/src/PacketMethods.h` into a 128-byte stack buffer, then dispatched via `SendOpcode(ENB_OPCODE_00B4_SUBPARTS, subparts, index)` where `index` is the accumulated byte count. For Terran (race=0) the handler walks the default race-switch arm at PlayerClass.cpp:1016-1038 and skips the Trader/Terran/upgrade≥5 branch at PlayerClass.cpp:1026-1037 (our Warrior account is not Trader, so the inner if-guard fails). Verified payload composition: 4 (GameID via AddData<long>) + 4 (sentinel literal 4 via AddData<long>) + 3 ("~01" via AddDataS) + 1 (raw index++) + 4 (profession via AddData<long>) + 3 ("~02" via AddDataS) + 1 (raw index++) + 4 (wing via AddData<long>) + 10 ("~02/~03_01" via AddDataS) + 1 (raw index++) + 4 (engine via AddData<long>) + 10 ("~02/~03_02" via AddDataS) + 1 (raw index++) + 4 (engine via AddData<long>) = **54 bytes**. Primary source citation: the Phase K Wave 12 `AddData<long>` 4-byte-LE specialization at `server/src/PacketMethods.h:37-49` and the Phase K Wave 12 inline-write comment at `server/src/PlayerClass.cpp:1028-1030` (`*((int32_t*) &subparts[4]) = ntohl(6)` — overwrites the slot AddData put there at offset 4 with a 4-byte int32_t) are themselves project attestations that wire offsets 0/4/12/20/35/50 are 4-byte slots — independent code paths reaching identical 4-byte-slot assumptions are a primary-source statement of the byte-exact invariant. Wave 90 is **+0** because 0x00B4 is already counted by Wave 35. **+0 ratchet (0x00B4 already counted by Wave 35); coverage stays at 104/207 (50.2%).**
